issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle does not announce state changes via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: The click handler `_onTriggerClick` (and the keyboard handler `_onTriggerKeydown`) open and close dropdown panels, but no `liveRegion.announce` call is made to inform screen‑reader users of the change. This violates WCAG 2.2 SC 4.1.3 (ARIA live region announcements for dynamic UI changes).
issuesFound.fix: After calling `_openInstance(inst)` or `_closeInstance(inst)`, add `liveRegion.announce(inst.open ? 'Menu opened' : 'Menu closed');` (or a more specific message).

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter view‑mode toggle button does not announce layout change via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: Changing the view mode updates the grid’s CSS classes but no live‑region announcement is made. Users of screen readers receive no feedback that the view has switched.
issuesFound.fix: After applying the new class, call `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.viewModeChanged?.replace('{mode}', mode) || \`View changed to \${mode}\`);`.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter sort `<select>` change does not announce the new sort order via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onSortChange` handler updates the sorting of products but never announces the change. This leaves screen‑reader users unaware of the new ordering.
issuesFound.fix: After updating `_productFilterState.sortKey` and `_productFilterState.sortDir`, add `liveRegion.announce(\`Products sorted by \${_productFilterState.sortKey} \${_productFilterState.sortDir}\`);`.

issuesFound.title: Search input (`#search-input`) lacks `aria-controls` linking to the suggestion list
issuesFound.explanation: The search component creates a live‑region status element and a suggestion list, but the input does not have an `aria-controls` attribute pointing to the list (`[id="...-listbox"]`). Without this, assistive technologies cannot associate the suggestions with the input.
issuesFound.fix: In `siteSearch.init`, after mounting the listbox, add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-controls', suggestionListRenderer._listId);`.

issuesFound.title: Search component does not announce result‑count updates via a dedicated live region
issuesFound.explanation: `_updateStatus` updates the status element’s text, but the element is not given `role="status"` or `aria-live="polite"` at creation time, so changes may not be announced.
issuesFound.fix: When creating `_statusEl`, set `setAttr(_statusEl, 'role', 'status'); setAttr(_statusEl, 'aria-live', 'polite');`.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter view‑mode toggle button lacks an accessible name (`aria-label` or visible text)
issuesFound.explanation: The view‑mode buttons are identified only by `data-view-mode` and may contain only an icon. Without visible text or an `aria-label`, screen‑reader users cannot discern their purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure each button includes descriptive text or `setAttr(btn, 'aria-label', mode === 'grid' ? 'Grid view' : 'List view');`.

issuesFound.title: Sort `<select>` element lacks an accessible label (`aria-label` or associated `<label>`)
issuesFound.explanation: The sort control is selected via `SELECTORS.sortSelect` but no label is enforced in the script. If the markup omits a `<label>`, the control is inaccessible.
issuesFound.fix: After obtaining `_sortSelect`, add `if (!_sortSelect.id) _sortSelect.id = srUtils.ensureId(_sortSelect, 'sort-select'); setAttr(_sortSelect, 'aria-label', 'Sort products');`.

issuesFound.title: Filter‑tab buttons (`[data-filter-value]`) lack proper ARIA tab roles (`role="tab"` and `role="tablist"` on the container)
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tabs are made keyboard‑navigable via roving tabindex, but they do not carry the required `role="tablist"` on the container and `role="tab"` on each button, nor `aria-controls` linking to the associated panel. This prevents AT from recognizing the tab interface.
issuesFound.fix: In `productFilter.init`, after locating `_tabsEl`, add `setAttr(_tabsEl, 'role', 'tablist');` and for each tab button `setAttr(btn, 'role', 'tab'); setAttr(btn, 'aria-controls', srUtils.ensureId(panelEl, 'filter-panel'));`.

issuesFound.title: Filter‑tab buttons do not announce activation via a live region when a new category is selected
issuesFound.explanation: Changing the active filter category updates the product list, but no live‑region announcement informs the user which category is now active. This violates SC 4.1.3.
issuesFound.fix: In `_onTabClick`, after updating `_productFilterState.activeCategory` and calling `applyFilters()`, add `liveRegion.announce(\`Filter set to \${_productFilterState.activeCategory}\`);`.

issuesFound.title: Toggle function `mobileNav.toggle` does not set **aria‑controls** on the hamburger button when the drawer element lacks an `id`.
issuesFound.explanation: The button controls the navigation drawer, but without `aria-controls` screen‑reader users cannot discover the relationship.
issuesFound.fix:
Add an `id` to the drawer (e.g., `id="nav-drawer"`) and ensure the trigger receives `setAttribute('aria-controls','nav-drawer')` (or generate a unique id in the init routine).

---

issuesFound.title: Toggle function `dropdownMenu._openInstance` hides/shows a dropdown panel but never adds **aria‑controls** to the trigger when the panel has no `id`.
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger button lacks a programmatic reference to the controlled menu, breaking the disclosure pattern.
issuesFound.fix:
If the panel has no `id`, generate one (`panel.id = 'dropdown-' + shortId()`) and call `setAttr(trigger,'aria-controls',panel.id)` during registration.

---

issuesFound.title: Toggle function `pricing.setPeriod` updates the visual state of the billing‑period buttons but does **not** set **aria‑pressed** on the button that acts as a simple on/off switch (`_toggleEl`).
issuesFound.explanation: The switch changes between monthly and annual billing; without `aria-pressed` assistive technologies cannot convey its state.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(_toggleEl,'aria-pressed', String(isYear))` whenever the period changes (mirroring the existing `aria-checked` handling).

---

issuesFound.title: Toggle function `productFilter._viewToggle` (the “grid/list” view buttons) updates CSS classes but does **not** set **aria‑pressed** on the buttons when they are activated.
issuesFound.explanation: Screen‑reader users cannot know which view mode is active.
issuesFound.fix:
After toggling, call `setAttr(btn,'aria-pressed', String(isActive))` for each view‑mode button.

---

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update `productFilter.applyFilters` changes the visible product count and empty‑state message but does **not** write to a dedicated live‑region for the count change. It only uses `liveRegion.announce`, which may be overridden by other announcements.
issuesFound.explanation: If another component announces later, the product‑count update can be lost to the user.
issuesFound.fix:
Create a persistent `<div role="status" aria-live="polite" id="product-count-status">` and update its text (`textContent = …`) each time the count changes.

---

issuesFound.title: Validation function `contactForm._onSubmit` marks fields invalid via `inlineValidator.validate`, which sets `aria-invalid`, but when the form is successfully submitted it never **clears** `aria-invalid` on fields that were previously invalid.
issuesFound.explanation: Assistive technologies may continue to announce errors after the form is valid.
issuesFound.fix:
After a successful submit, iterate the form’s inputs and call `srUtils.markValid(field)` (or remove `aria-invalid`) for each.

---

issuesFound.title: Visibility‑toggle function `mobileNav._close` hides the overlay (`_overlay`) by adding a CSS class and later setting `hidden`, but never sets **aria‑hidden** on the overlay element.
issuesFound.explanation: When the overlay is visible, it remains exposed to AT even though it blocks interaction with the page.
issuesFound.fix:
When showing the overlay, set `_overlay.removeAttribute('aria-hidden')`; when hiding, set `_overlay.setAttribute('aria-hidden','true')`.

---

issuesFound.title: Visibility‑toggle function `pricing._updateSavingsMessage` hides the savings banner with `setAttribute('hidden')` but does **not** set **aria‑hidden**.
issuesFound.explanation: Screen readers may still read the hidden banner.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `_savingsMsg.setAttribute('aria-hidden','true')` when hidden and remove it when shown.

---

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update `testimonialsCarousel._onPrev/_onNext` changes the visible slide and announces it via `liveRegion.announce`, but the slide number is not written to a dedicated live‑region element that is always present (the carousel lacks a persistent status region).
issuesFound.explanation: If another script writes to the live region shortly after, the slide announcement can be overwritten.
issuesFound.fix:
Add a persistent `
` inside the carousel and update its `textContent` with the slide announcement instead of calling `liveRegion.announce` directly. ---

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update `productFilter._syncUrl` modifies the URL hash and query parameters but does **not** announce the navigation change to screen‑reader users (e.g., when the filter changes the page URL).
issuesFound.explanation: Users relying on AT may not be aware that the page state has changed.
issuesFound.fix:
Add a hidden live‑region (`
`) and update its text (e.g., “Filter applied: Category X”) after each URL sync. ---

issuesFound.title: Toggle function `fileUploadField._addFile` adds files to the upload list and announces via `liveRegion.announce`, but the upload list itself lacks an **aria‑label** or **role="list"** to convey its purpose.
issuesFound.explanation: Screen‑reader users cannot identify the list of pending uploads.
issuesFound.fix:
When creating the list (`_listEl`), set `setAttr(_listEl,'role','list')` and `setAttr(_listEl,'aria-label','Files pending upload')`.

---

issuesFound.title: Visibility‑toggle function `productFilter._updateEmptyState` hides the empty‑state element with `setAttribute('hidden')` but does not set **aria‑hidden**.
issuesFound.explanation: The element remains perceivable to AT when hidden.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `_emptyState.setAttribute('aria-hidden','true')` when hidden and remove it when shown.

---

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update `pricing._updatePlanCard` changes the price text but does **not** write the new price to a live‑region for screen‑reader users (the price element receives an `aria-label`, but the label is not announced because no live region is used).
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period changes, users may not hear the updated price.
issuesFound.fix: Add a hidden live‑region inside each price container (``) and update its `textContent` with the new price whenever `setAttr(plan.priceDisplay, ...)` is called. ---

issuesFound.title: Toggle function `multiStepWizard._onNext` validates the current step and, on success, moves to the next step but does **not** announce the step change via a dedicated live‑region (it only updates `aria-selected` on tabs).
issuesFound.explanation: Screen‑reader users may not know that the wizard has advanced.
issuesFound.fix:
Update a persistent status region (`
`) with a message like “Step 2 of 4: Payment details”. ---

fpTitles: Toggle function `mobileNav.toggle` does not set **aria‑controls** on the hamburger button when the drawer element lacks an `id`.
Toggle function `dropdownMenu._openInstance` hides/shows a dropdown panel but never adds **aria‑controls** to the trigger when the panel has no `id`.
Toggle function `pricing.setPeriod` updates the visual state of the billing‑period buttons but does **not** set **aria‑pressed** on the button that acts as a simple on/off switch (`_toggleEl`).
Toggle function `productFilter._viewToggle` (the “grid/list” view buttons) updates CSS classes but does **not** set **aria‑pressed** on the buttons when they are activated.

Dynamic content update `productFilter.applyFilters` changes the visible product count and empty‑state message but does **not** write to a dedicated live‑region for the count change. It only uses `liveRegion.announce`, which may be overridden by other announcements.

Validation function `contactForm._onSubmit` marks fields invalid via `inlineValidator.validate`, which sets `aria-invalid`, but when the form is successfully submitted it never **clears** `aria-invalid` on fields that were previously invalid.
Visibility‑toggle function `mobileNav._close` hides the overlay (`_overlay`) by adding a CSS class and later setting `hidden`, but never sets **aria‑hidden** on the overlay element.
Visibility‑toggle function `pricing._updateSavingsMessage` hides the savings banner with `setAttribute('hidden')` but does **not** set **aria‑hidden**.

Dynamic content update `testimonialsCarousel._onPrev/_onNext` changes the visible slide and announces it via `liveRegion.announce`, but the slide number is not written to a dedicated live‑region element that is always present (the carousel lacks a persistent status region).

Dynamic content update `productFilter._syncUrl` modifies the URL hash and query parameters but does **not** announce the navigation change to screen‑reader users (e.g., when the filter changes the page URL).

Toggle function `fileUploadField._addFile` adds files to the upload list and announces via `liveRegion.announce`, but the upload list itself lacks an **aria‑label** or **role="list"** to convey its purpose.
Visibility‑toggle function `productFilter._updateEmptyState` hides the empty‑state element with `setAttribute('hidden')` but does not set **aria‑hidden**.

Dynamic content update `pricing._updatePlanCard` changes the price text but does **not** write the new price to a live‑region for screen‑reader users (the price element receives an `aria-label`, but the label is not announced because no live region is used).

Toggle function `multiStepWizard._onNext` validates the current step and, on success, moves to the next step but does **not** announce the step change via a dedicated live‑region (it only updates `aria-selected` on tabs).

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer does not update aria‑hidden when opened
issuesFound.explanation: In mobileNav.open() the drawer element is shown by removing the “hidden” attribute and adding CSS classes, but aria‑hidden is never set to false. Screen readers therefore still treat the drawer as hidden.
issuesFound.fix:
After making the drawer visible, add setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'false'); and when closing add setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); in mobileNav.close().

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer does not update aria‑hidden when closed
issuesFound.explanation: mobileNav.close() hides the drawer by adding CSS classes and setting hidden, but it never restores aria‑hidden to true, so the element remains exposed to AT after being hidden.
issuesFound.fix:
In mobileNav.close() add setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); before or after adding the hidden attribute.

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation overlay lacks aria‑hidden when shown
issuesFound.explanation: When the overlay is shown (the “hidden” attribute is removed) the script does not set aria‑hidden to false, leaving assistive technologies unaware that the overlay is now part of the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix:
After removing hidden from _overlay, call setAttr(_overlay, 'aria-hidden', 'false'); and set it back to true when the overlay is hidden.

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation overlay lacks aria‑hidden when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The overlay is hidden by adding the hidden attribute, but aria‑hidden is never set to true, so the element may still be announced by screen readers.
issuesFound.fix:
In the hide branch of mobileNav.close() add setAttr(_overlay, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); after adding the hidden attribute.

---

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter empty‑state element does not toggle aria‑hidden when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: _productFilter._updateEmptyState() adds or removes the hidden attribute on the empty‑state element but never updates aria‑hidden, so the element’s visibility is not communicated to AT.
issuesFound.fix:
When hiding the empty‑state element, call setAttr(_emptyState, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); and when showing, call setAttr(_emptyState, 'aria-hidden', 'false');.

---

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter empty‑state element does not toggle aria‑hidden when shown
issuesFound.explanation: The same function shows the empty‑state element by removing hidden without setting aria‑hidden to false, leaving screen readers unaware that the message is now visible.
issuesFound.fix:
After _emptyState.removeAttribute('hidden'); add setAttr(_emptyState, 'aria-hidden', 'false');.

---

issuesFound.title: Search suggestion list does not update aria‑hidden when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: suggestionListRenderer.mount() creates the listbox with a hidden attribute but never sets aria‑hidden to true, so AT may still expose the listbox when it is hidden.
issuesFound.fix:
When initializing the list, add setAttr(_listEl, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); and ensure aria‑hidden is set to false when the list is shown.

---

issuesFound.title: Search suggestion list does not update aria‑hidden when shown
issuesFound.explanation: suggestionListRenderer.show() removes the hidden attribute but does not set aria‑hidden to false, leaving the listbox invisible to AT until it is manually focused.
issuesFound.fix:
In show(), after _removeAttribute('hidden') add setAttr(_listEl, 'aria-hidden', 'false'); so the listbox becomes perceivable.

---

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle button lacks aria‑controls to reference the region it updates
issuesFound.explanation: The billing‑period toggle ( [data-billing-toggle] ) changes the displayed prices of all plan cards, but it does not expose an aria‑controls relationship to the container of those cards. Assistive technologies cannot infer which region is being affected.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-controls', _pricingContainer.id); where _pricingContainer is the element that holds the pricing cards (e.g., document.querySelector('.pricing-section')). This creates a clear relationship between the toggle and the content it controls.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open does not set aria‑expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: _dropdownMenu._openInstance removes the hidden attribute from the panel and adds the open class, yet it never sets aria‑expanded on the associated trigger button. The button remains announced as collapsed.
issuesFound.fix:
Inside _openInstance add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` when the menu is opened.

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open does not set aria‑expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.open adds the “open” class and animates the panel, but it does not set aria‑expanded on the question button. Assistive technologies therefore think the item is still collapsed.
issuesFound.fix:
After opening, call `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` to reflect the expanded state.

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close actions lack live‑region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu’s _openInstance and _closeInstance functions change visibility but never write a message to a live region. Users are not informed when a menu opens or closes.
issuesFound.fix:
After opening, add `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');` and after closing, `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');` or use a dedicated status element with aria‑live.

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer does not update aria‑hidden when shown/hidden
issuesFound.explanation: mobileNav.open removes hidden from the drawer but never removes aria‑hidden, and mobileNav.close adds hidden without setting aria‑hidden to true. The drawer remains invisible to AT when hidden and incorrectly exposed when shown.
issuesFound.fix:
In open, add `_drawer.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');` and in close, add `_drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');`.

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panel does not update aria‑hidden when shown/hidden
issuesFound.explanation: _openInstance removes hidden but does not clear aria‑hidden, and _closeInstance adds hidden without setting aria‑hidden to true, leaving the panel’s accessibility state out of sync with its visual state.
issuesFound.fix:
In _openInstance, add `inst.panel.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');` and in _closeInstance, add `inst.panel.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');`.

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panel does not update aria‑hidden when shown/hidden
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.open removes hidden but does not remove aria‑hidden, while faqAccordion.close adds hidden without setting aria‑hidden to true. The panel’s accessibility state is therefore inaccurate.
issuesFound.fix:
In open, add `item.panel.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');` and in close, add `item.panel.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');`.

---

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form email validation does not set aria‑invalid on error
issuesFound.explanation: In newsletterForm._onSubmit the email field is validated with validateField, but when the validation fails the code only shows a status message; it never marks the <input> with aria‑invalid="true". Screen readers cannot detect the error.
issuesFound.fix:
After a failed validation, call `srUtils.markInvalid(emailInput, errorMessageElement);` or `emailInput.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` and clear it on success.

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu close does not set aria‑hidden on the panel (duplicate of Issue 6 but for the close path)
issuesFound.explanation: The _closeInstance function adds hidden to the panel but does not set aria‑hidden to true, leaving the hidden state invisible to AT.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `inst.panel.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');` inside _closeInstance after hiding the panel.

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation close does not set aria‑hidden on the drawer (duplicate of Issue 5 but for the close path)
issuesFound.explanation: mobileNav.close adds hidden to the drawer without setting aria‑hidden to true, so the drawer remains perceivable to AT when it is visually hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Add `_drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');` in the close function after adding the hidden attribute.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation “open” function does not set aria‑expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function in the mobileNav module adds the CSS class that makes the drawer visible, but it never updates the aria‑expanded state on the hamburger button. Screen‑reader users therefore cannot know that the navigation is now expanded.
issuesFound.fix:
After the drawer is shown, add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); (e.g. just before _lockScroll(); or after addClass(_drawer,…)).  

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu “open” instance does not set aria‑expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: In dropdownMenu._openInstance() the panel is shown and the CSS class is added, yet the trigger button’s aria‑expanded attribute is never set to true. This leaves the state ambiguous for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
Inside _openInstance() add setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); immediately after the panel is made visible.  

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer is not marked aria‑hidden when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The drawer element is hidden via CSS classes, but no aria‑hidden attribute is applied. Assistive technologies may still expose the hidden navigation to users.
issuesFound.fix:
In mobileNav.close() add setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); and in mobileNav.open() remove that attribute (or set to 'false').  

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panels are not marked aria‑hidden when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The panel is hidden by adding the hidden attribute, but aria‑hidden is not set. Some ATs rely on aria‑hidden to remove content from the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix:
In _dropdownMenu._closeInstance() add setAttr(inst.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); when hiding, and remove the attribute when showing (in _openInstance()).  

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close actions are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: The module emits custom events but does not provide a screen‑reader announcement of the menu state change. Users who cannot see the visual cue receive no feedback.
issuesFound.fix:
After opening, call liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened'); and after closing, liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed'); or use a dedicated <div role="status" aria-live="polite"> to receive the messages.  

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion does not announce state changes via live region on toggle (duplicate of missing aria‑expanded)
issuesFound.explanation: Although the module calls liveRegion.announce when an item is opened, the announcement text is constructed from the question text only. When the answer is long, the user may miss important content.
issuesFound.fix:
Include a brief summary or indicate that the answer is now visible, e.g., liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} – answer expanded`); and similarly for collapse.  

---

fpTitles: Mobile navigation drawer is not marked aria‑hidden when closed

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open missing aria‑expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open()` function toggles the mobile navigation drawer but never sets `aria‑expanded="true"` on the trigger button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware that the menu is now expanded.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after the drawer is opened.

---

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close does not set aria‑hidden on drawer
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is closed, `mobileNav.close()` removes CSS classes and hides the drawer but does not set `aria‑hidden="true"` on the drawer element, so the hidden state is not communicated to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
After hiding the drawer, call `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');`.

---

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance does not set aria‑hidden on panel
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown panel is opened, the function removes the `hidden` attribute but does not set `aria‑hidden="false"` on the panel, leaving the accessibility tree incorrectly indicating the panel is hidden.
issuesFound.fix:
After removing `hidden`, call `setAttr(inst.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'false');`.

---

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance missing aria‑hidden on panel
issuesFound.explanation: The `_closeInstance()` function hides the dropdown panel by adding a CSS class and setting `hidden`, but it never sets `aria‑hidden="true"` on the panel, so assistive technologies are not informed that the panel is hidden.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(inst.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` when closing the dropdown.

---

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close actions lack live‑region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing dropdown menus update visibility but do not provide an announcement via a live region, leaving users of screen readers unaware of the state change.
issuesFound.fix:
After opening, call `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');` and after closing, `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');`.

---

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open missing aria‑expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The `open(item)` function expands an FAQ answer but never sets `aria‑expanded="true"` on the trigger button, so the expanded state is not exposed to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` when opening the FAQ item.

---

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open does not set aria‑hidden on panel
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ answer panel is opened, the function removes the `hidden` attribute but does not set `aria‑hidden="false"` on the panel, leaving the accessibility tree inconsistent.
issuesFound.fix:
After removing `hidden`, call `setAttr(item.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'false');`.

---

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator._renderResult()` function marks fields as invalid visually but never adds `aria-invalid="true"` to the input element, violating WCAG 2.1 SC 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix:
When `result.valid` is false, call `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`.

---

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not remove aria‑invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When a field becomes valid, the validator clears visual error styles but does not remove or set `aria-invalid="false"` on the input, leaving stale invalid state for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: In the valid branch, call `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');` or `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panels hidden/shown do not set aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: Panels are hidden via the hidden attribute only; aria‑hidden is not toggled, so hidden content stays in the accessibility tree.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation:
When a field fails validation the code calls srUtils.markInvalid which only adds aria‑describedby and a CSS class; aria‑invalid is never set, so AT cannot detect the error state.  
  Fix (inside _ renderResult when !result.valid):  
```js
if (!result.valid) {
  setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true'); // add this line
  …
}
```

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not clear aria‑invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation:
When a field becomes valid the code removes the error CSS class but never removes aria‑invalid, leaving the field permanently marked as invalid to AT.  
  Fix (inside _ renderResult when result.valid):  
```js
if (result.valid) {
  removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID); // add this line
  …
}
```

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open/close actions are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing the mobile menu updates visual state but does not provide an audible cue for screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix:
add after the state change:  
```js
liveRegion.announce(_open ? STRINGS.navOpen : STRINGS.navClosed);
```

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close actions are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: Users are not informed when a dropdown opens or closes.
issuesFound.fix:
after toggling, call:  
```js
liveRegion.announce(_open ? 'Dropdown opened' : 'Dropdown closed');
```

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close actions are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: No audible feedback when a question is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix:
after state change, call:  
```js
liveRegion.announce(_open ? `${item.question} – answer expanded` : `${item.question} – answer collapsed`);
```

fpTitles: Mobile navigation trigger missing initial aria‑expanded attribute
Mobile navigation drawer hidden/shown does not set aria‑hidden
Dropdown menu close does not update aria‑expanded on trigger
Dropdown menu panels hidden/shown do not set aria‑hidden
FAQ accordion panels hidden/shown do not set aria‑hidden

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The functions _openInstance and _closeInstance in the dropdown menu controller change the visibility of the dropdown panel (add/remove CSS classes, set/remove hidden attribute) but never update the aria‑expanded state on the trigger button. This leaves screen‑reader users unaware of the menu’s open/closed state.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle missing aria‑controls and initial aria‑expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger (the element matched by SELECTORS.navToggle) is used to open/close the off‑canvas drawer, but it never receives an aria‑controls attribute that points to the drawer’s id, nor is aria‑expanded initialised on page load. Assistive technologies therefore cannot associate the button with the region it controls or know its default state.

issuesFound.title: Form validation does not set aria‑invalid on error (required field)
issuesFound.explanation: In the newsletter form validation (module newsletterForm), the email field is validated with validateField but the code never calls setAttribute('aria-invalid','true') when the field is invalid, nor does it clear the attribute when the field becomes valid. This prevents screen readers from announcing the error state.

issuesFound.title: Contact form validation does not set aria‑invalid on error
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form uses inlineValidator.validate to mark fields as invalid, but the validation rules do not include a step that sets aria‑invalid='true' on the offending inputs. Consequently, AT users receive no programmatic indication of the error.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle button missing aria‑pressed state update
issuesFound.explanation: The simple on/off billing‑period toggle (element [data-billing-toggle]) changes its visual state but never updates aria‑pressed to reflect the current selection (monthly vs. annual). Assistive technologies cannot convey the toggle’s state.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update (product count badge) not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: After filtering products, productFilter.applyFilters updates the count badge text but does not write the new count to an aria‑live region. Users of assistive technology are not informed that the number of visible products has changed.

issuesFound.title: Search suggestions list lacks role="listbox" and aria‑activedescendant updates on keyboard navigation
issuesFound.explanation: The suggestion list created by suggestionListRenderer adds role="listbox" but the individual suggestion items are not given role="option" and the input does not update aria‑activedescendant when the user navigates with arrow keys. This reduces accessibility of the autocomplete component.

issuesFound.title: Toast notifications lack role="status" or aria‑live attributes
issuesFound.explanation: The toast manager creates toast elements with role="alert" but does not set aria‑live or role="status" to ensure screen readers announce the message. While role="alert" is sufficient for most AT, the container <div class="toast"> should also have aria‑live="assertive" to guarantee announcement.

fpTitles: Contact form validation does not set aria‑invalid on error
Pricing period toggle button missing aria‑pressed state update
Search suggestions list lacks role="listbox" and aria‑activedescendant updates on keyboard navigation

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing `aria‑expanded`
issuesFound.explanation: The element referenced by `SELECTORS.navToggle` (the hamburger button) never receives an `aria-expanded` attribute. The button opens and closes the mobile drawer, but screen readers have no programmatic indication of its state, violating WCAG 4.1.2 (Disclosure button missing `aria‑expanded`).
issuesFound.fix:
In `mobileNav.init()` set `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`. Then in `mobileNav.open()` call `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')` and in `mobileNav.close()` call `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing `aria‑controls`
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button controls the drawer element (`_drawer`) but never receives an `aria-controls` attribute that points to the drawer’s `id`. Assistive technologies cannot associate the control with the region it toggles.
issuesFound.fix:
After the drawer is registered (`_drawer = qs(SELECTORS.navDrawer …)`), ensure the drawer has an `id` (e.g., `srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-drawer')`) and then `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', _drawer.id)`.

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing `aria‑pressed`
issuesFound.explanation: The button acts as a two‑state toggle (open / closed) but never updates `aria-pressed`. This prevents screen‑reader users from perceiving the current state.
issuesFound.fix:
In `mobileNav.open()` add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', 'true')`; in `mobileNav.close()` add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', 'false')`.

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown panel hidden without `aria‑hidden`
issuesFound.explanation: `_closeInstance()` hides the panel by adding a CSS class and setting the `hidden` attribute, but it never sets `aria-hidden="true"`. The element remains in the accessibility tree while visually hidden.
issuesFound.fix:
After `inst.panel.setAttribute('hidden', '')` also call `setAttr(inst.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true')`. In `_openInstance()` remove the attribute (`inst.panel.removeAttribute('aria-hidden')`).

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger missing `aria‑expanded`
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ items are parsed in `faqRegistry.parse()` and receive `aria-controls`, but the trigger button never receives an `aria-expanded` attribute. Opening/closing a FAQ panel does not update this state, breaking WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix:
In `faqAccordion.open(item)` add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; in `faqAccordion.close(item)` add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`. Also initialise each trigger with `aria-expanded="false"` during parsing.

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panel hidden without `aria‑hidden`
issuesFound.explanation: When a FAQ item is closed, the panel receives the `hidden` attribute but not `aria-hidden="true"`. The panel stays in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix:
In `faqAccordion.close(item)` after `item.panel.setAttribute('hidden', '')` also call `setAttr(item.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true')`. In `faqAccordion.open(item)` remove the attribute (`item.panel.removeAttribute('aria-hidden')`).

---

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter count badge updates not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: `_updateCountBadge(count)` updates the numeric badge but does not send an announcement to a live region. Users of assistive technology are not informed when the number of visible products changes after filtering.
issuesFound.fix:
After updating the badge text, call `liveRegion.announce(`${count} product${count !== 1 ? 's' : ''} shown`)`. Ensure the live region element exists (e.g., create one with `role="status"` if not present).

---

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle does not announce state change
issuesFound.explanation: `pricing.setPeriod()` updates the UI (price numbers, badges) but never announces the new billing period to screen readers. Users cannot know whether “monthly” or “annual” pricing is active.
issuesFound.fix:
After the price update, add `liveRegion.announce(`Billing period switched to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label}`)`.

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer hidden without `aria‑hidden`
issuesFound.explanation: When the drawer is closed (`mobileNav.close()`), the drawer element is hidden via CSS classes but never receives `aria-hidden="true"`. The drawer remains in the accessibility tree while invisible.
issuesFound.fix:
In `mobileNav.close()` after removing the open class, call `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true')`. In `mobileNav.open()` remove the attribute (`_drawer.removeAttribute('aria-hidden')`).

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation overlay hidden without `aria‑hidden`
issuesFound.explanation: The overlay element (`_overlay`) is shown/hidden with CSS but never receives `aria-hidden`. Assistive technologies may still expose it.
issuesFound.fix:
Mirror the drawer fix: when hiding the overlay set `setAttr(_overlay, 'aria-hidden', 'true')`; when showing remove the attribute.

---

issuesFound.title: View‑mode toggle button missing `aria‑pressed` on initial load
issuesFound.explanation: The view‑mode buttons (`data-view-mode`) are given `aria-pressed` only when the user clicks them. On page load the button representing the persisted view mode does not have the correct `aria-pressed` state, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the current view.
issuesFound.fix:
After reading the persisted view mode in `productFilter.init()`, call `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(btn.getAttribute('data-view-mode') === persistedMode))` for each view‑mode button, and ensure the default button is also set.

---

issuesFound.title: Search results page does not announce filtered results
issuesFound.explanation: `searchResultsHighlighter.highlight()` highlights terms but does not announce the number of results found. Users navigating with a screen reader receive no feedback after a search.
issuesFound.fix:
After highlighting, call `liveRegion.announce(`${resultCount} result${resultCount !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`)` where `resultCount` is the number of matched items.

---

issuesFound.title: Pricing comparison table column highlight does not announce selection
issuesFound.explanation: `planComparisonHighlighter.highlightColumn(planId)` visually highlights a column but does not provide an audible cue. Keyboard users who move focus to a column header receive no announcement.
issuesFound.fix: After highlighting, call `liveRegion.announce(`Comparison column for ${planId} highlighted`)` or add `aria-live="polite"` to the column header and update its text content accordingly.

fpTitles: Mobile navigation drawer hidden without `aria‑hidden`
Mobile navigation overlay hidden without `aria‑hidden`

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance function (lines 2152‑2158) shows/hides a dropdown panel but never updates the aria‑expanded state on the trigger button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the menu’s state.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', String(inst.open)) inside _openInstance (and the corresponding false value in _closeInstance).

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: In faqAccordion.open (lines 3401‑3409) the trigger button’s aria‑expanded attribute is never set when a FAQ item is opened, so assistive technologies cannot convey the open/closed state.
issuesFound.fix: Call setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true') when opening and 'false' when closing.

issuesFound.title: Site‑search input missing aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search <input> (lines 1798‑1805) is given role="combobox" and aria‑autocomplete, but lacks aria‑expanded, which is required to indicate whether the suggestion list is visible.
issuesFound.fix: When the suggestion panel opens, set setAttr(searchInput, 'aria-expanded', 'true') and set it to 'false' when closed.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The period toggle buttons (e.g., [data-period]) are used as two‑state controls but never receive aria‑pressed, violating the ARIA button pattern.
issuesFound.fix: In _setPeriod or the button click handler, add setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive)) for each button.

issuesFound.title: Simple billing‑toggle switch missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The [data-billing-toggle] element acts as a switch (on/off) but does not have aria‑pressed or aria‑checked to convey its state.
issuesFound.fix: After toggling, call setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', String(isYear)) or use aria-checked.

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav hamburger button missing aria‑controls
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger .nav-toggle button opens #nav-drawer but never declares aria‑controls pointing to the drawer’s id, so screen readers cannot associate the control with the region it toggles.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.init add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'nav-drawer')).

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not set aria‑invalid
issuesFound.explanation: inlineValidator.validate (validates fields) marks fields visually but never sets aria‑invalid on invalid inputs, preventing AT from announcing errors.
issuesFound.fix: In _ renderResult when result.valid is false, call setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true'); on success, remove the attribute.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter‑form validation does not set aria‑invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter form validates the email address manually but never applies aria‑invalid to the <input> when the address is invalid, so screen readers miss the error state.
issuesFound.fix: After the regex test fails, add setAttr(emailInput, 'aria-invalid', 'true') and remove it on success.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle button lacks aria‑pressed (and aria‑expanded) for screen‑reader feedback
issuesFound.explanation: The period toggle (e.g., monthly/annual) is a two‑state button but only receives a CSS .active class; no ARIA state is communicated.
issuesFound.fix: In _setPeriod or the click handler, set setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive)) and optionally aria-expanded.

issuesFound.title: Site‑search suggestion list items lack aria‑selected updates
issuesFound.explanation: When navigating suggestions with arrow keys, the code updates CSS .active but does not toggle aria‑selected on the [role="option"] elements, which is required for assistive technology to know the current selection.
issuesFound.fix: In suggestionListRenderer.setActiveIndex add setAttr(option, 'aria-selected', String(isActive)) for each option.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panels not marked aria‑hidden when collapsed
issuesFound.explanation: When a FAQ item is closed, the panel receives hidden attribute but never receives aria‑hidden="true", leaving the element still perceivable to AT.
issuesFound.fix: In close add setAttr(item.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); and remove it on open.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown‑menu panels lack aria‑hidden when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown panel is hidden via hidden attribute but does not receive aria‑hidden, so screen readers may still read its content.
issuesFound.fix: In _closeInstance add setAttr(inst.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); and remove it in _openInstance.

fpTitles: Newsletter‑form validation does not set aria‑invalid
Pricing period toggle button lacks aria‑pressed (and aria‑expanded) for screen‑reader feedback

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger missing aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The button that toggles each dropdown menu is given aria-controls but never receives an aria‑expanded attribute, so screen‑reader users cannot know whether the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: In register() add setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false') when the dropdown is initialized and update it in _openInstance() and _closeInstance() to reflect the open state.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown panel not updated with aria‑hidden on open
issuesFound.explanation: _openInstance() removes the hidden attribute from the panel but does not remove aria‑hidden, leaving the panel hidden from assistive technologies even when visible.
issuesFound.fix: After inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden') add inst.panel.removeAttribute('aria-hidden') or set aria-hidden="false".

issuesFound.title: Dropdown panel not updated with aria‑hidden on close
issuesFound.explanation: _closeInstance() adds the hidden attribute but never sets aria‑hidden="true", so AT may still treat the panel as visible.
issuesFound.fix: After inst.panel.setAttribute('hidden','') add inst.panel.setAttribute('aria-hidden','true').

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer missing aria‑hidden updates
issuesFound.explanation: mobileNav.open() removes the drawer’s hidden attribute but does not clear aria‑hidden; mobileNav.close() adds hidden but does not set aria‑hidden="true". This leaves the drawer’s accessibility state out of sync with its visual state.
issuesFound.fix: In open() add _drawer.removeAttribute('aria-hidden'); in close() add _drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden','true').

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger missing aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ question buttons are given aria-controls but never receive aria‑expanded, so screen‑reader users cannot tell whether the answer is shown.
issuesFound.fix: In faqAccordion.open() call setAttr(item.trigger,'aria-expanded','true') and in close() set it to 'false'.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panels not updated with aria‑hidden on open/close
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.open() removes the panel’s hidden attribute but does not remove aria‑hidden; close() adds hidden but does not set aria‑hidden="true".
issuesFound.fix: In open() add item.panel.removeAttribute('aria-hidden'); in close() add item.panel.setAttribute('aria-hidden','true').

issuesFound.title: Inline form validator does not set aria‑invalid on error
issuesFound.explanation: Validation errors are indicated only by CSS classes; the required aria‑invalid="true" attribute is never added, so AT cannot detect invalid fields.
issuesFound.fix: In _ renderResult() when result.valid is false call field.setAttribute('aria-invalid','true'); and when valid remove or set 'false'.

issuesFound.title: Inline form validator does not clear aria‑invalid on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: After a field becomes valid, the aria‑invalid attribute remains set, leaving AT to think the field is still invalid.
issuesFound.fix: In _renderResult() when result.valid is true call field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid') or set 'false'.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion does not announce open/close via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing an FAQ item updates the UI but no live‑region announcement is made, so screen‑reader users receive no feedback.
issuesFound.fix: After changing the state, call liveRegion.announce( `… opened` ) or `… closed` with appropriate text.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu does not initialise aria‑expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown triggers receive aria-controls but never receive an initial aria‑expanded value (should be false when closed).
issuesFound.fix: In register() add setAttr(trigger,'aria-expanded','false') so the attribute exists before any interaction.

issuesFound.title: Button loading state missing aria‑busy
issuesFound.explanation: The <Button> used in NewsletterForm receives a loading prop (instead of the component’s isLoading prop), so aria‑busy is never set. Assistive technologies cannot know the button is busy, affecting screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 560: <Button type="submit" variant="primary" size="md" isLoading={isSubmitting} aria-disabled={isSubmitting} aria-describedby={statusId}>

issuesFound.title: Email field aria‑describedby points to non‑existent error element
issuesFound.explanation: In NewsletterForm the TextInput for the email address sets aria-describedby="newsletter-email-error", but no element with that id exists, so error messages are not announced to screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 540: <TextInput id="newsletter-email" type="email" name="email" placeholder="you@company.com" autoComplete="email" value={email.state.value} onChange={(ev) => email.onChange(ev.target.value)} onBlur={email.onBlur} required aria-required="true" invalid={email.state.touched && !!email.state.error} aria-describedby={email.state.touched && email.state.error ? "newsletter-email-error" : undefined} />

issuesFound.title: Name field aria‑describedby points to non‑existent error element
issuesFound.explanation: In ContactForm the TextInput for “Full name” sets aria-describedby="contact-name-error", but no element with that id exists, so validation errors are not conveyed to AT users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 680: <TextInput id="contact-name" name="name" type="text" autoComplete="name" required aria-required="true" value={name.state.value} onChange={(ev) => name.onChange(ev.target.value)} onBlur={name.onBlur} invalid={name.state.touched && !!name.state.error} aria-describedby={name.state.touched && name.state.error ? "contact-name-error" : undefined} />

issuesFound.title: Subject field aria‑describedby points to non‑existent error element
issuesFound.explanation: In ContactForm the TextInput for “Subject” sets aria-describedby="contact-subject-error", but no element with that id exists, so AT users miss the validation feedback.
issuesFound.fix: Line 700: <TextInput id="contact-subject" name="subject" type="text" required aria-required="true" value={subject.state.value} onChange={(ev) => subject.onChange(ev.target.value)} onBlur={subject.onBlur} invalid={subject.state.touched && !!subject.state.error} aria-describedby={subject.state.touched && subject.state.error ? "contact-subject-error" : undefined} />

issuesFound.title: Message field aria‑describedby points to non‑existent error element
issuesFound.explanation: In ContactForm the Textarea for “Message” sets aria-describedby="contact-message-error", but no element with that id exists, so error messages are not announced to screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 710: <Textarea id="contact-message" name="message" rows={6} required aria-required="true" value={message.state.value} onChange={(ev) => message.onChange(ev.target.value)} onBlur={message.onBlur} invalid={message.state.touched && !!message.state.error} aria-describedby={["contact-message-counter", message.state.touched && message.state.error ? "contact-message-error" : ""].filter(Boolean).join(" ")} />

issuesFound.title: aria‑modal applied to non‑dialog element
issuesFound.explanation: The outer <div className="modal-overlay"> in Modal has aria‑modal="true" but does not have role="dialog". ARIA‑modal should be on the dialog element itself; applying it to the overlay can confuse assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1150: <div className="modal-overlay" onClick={onClose}> (remove aria-modal) 
  Line 1155 (or the dialog div): <div ref={dialogRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby={titleId} aria-describedby={description ? descId : undefined} className={['modal-dialog', `modal-dialog--${size}`].join(' ')} onClick={(ev) => ev.stopPropagation()}> (ensure aria‑modal remains here)

issuesFound.title: Modal backdrop <div> clickable without button role (HIGH)
issuesFound.explanation: The backdrop <div className="modal-overlay"> has an onClick handler to close the dialog but lacks role="button" (and an accessible name), making it inaccessible to keyboard and screen‑reader users.

issuesFound.title: Missing error message element referenced by aria-describedby in NewsletterForm email field
issuesFound.explanation: The `<TextInput>` for the newsletter email includes `aria-describedby="newsletter-email-error"` but no element with that id exists, so screen readers cannot locate the associated error message. This affects users relying on assistive technology when the field is invalid.

issuesFound.title: Missing error message element referenced by aria-describedby in ContactForm name field
issuesFound.explanation: The `<TextInput>` for “Full name” sets `aria-describedby="contact-name-error"` but no element with that id is rendered, leaving the error message inaccessible to AT users.

issuesFound.title: Missing error message element referenced by aria-describedby in ContactForm email field
issuesFound.explanation: The email `<TextInput>` includes `aria-describedby="contact-email-error"` but the corresponding error element is absent, preventing AT users from hearing the validation message.

issuesFound.title: Missing error message element referenced by aria-describedby in ContactForm subject field
issuesFound.explanation: The subject `<TextInput>` sets `aria-describedby="contact-subject-error"` without rendering an element with that id, so validation feedback is not announced to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: Missing error message element referenced by aria-describedby in ContactForm message textarea
issuesFound.explanation: The `<Textarea>` for the message includes `aria-describedby="contact-message-error"` but no matching element exists, so AT users cannot hear the error.

issuesFound.title: Loading button missing aria-busy attribute in NewsletterForm
issuesFound.explanation: The submit `<Button>` uses a non‑existent `loading` prop instead of the component’s `isLoading` prop, so `aria-busy` is never set. Assistive technologies cannot detect the loading state.

issuesFound.title: Loading button missing aria-busy attribute in ContactForm
issuesFound.explanation: The submit `<Button>` also uses the incorrect `loading` prop, preventing `aria-busy` from being applied and leaving the loading state invisible to AT users.

issuesFound.title: Clickable backdrop div missing role and keyboard support in Modal component
issuesFound.explanation: The `<div className="modal-overlay">` acts as a clickable element to close the dialog but has no `role="button"` and no keyboard handling, making it inaccessible to keyboard‑only users and screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1245: <div  
  Line 1245:   className="modal-overlay"  
  Line 1245:   role="button"               // added role  
  Line 1245:   aria-label="Close dialog"   // added accessible name  
  Line 1245:   tabIndex={0}                // make focusable  
  Line 1245:   onClick={onClose}  
  Line 1245:   onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); onClose(); } }} // keyboard support  
  Line 1245: >

issuesFound.title: Disclosure button missing aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The <button> that toggles the navigation dropdown (NavDropdown) does not have an aria‑expanded attribute that reflects the isOpen state, so screen‑reader users cannot know whether the submenu is open. This affects keyboard users trying to navigate the primary navigation.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle missing aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The button that opens and closes the off‑canvas mobile navigation (mobile‑nav‑toggle) lacks an aria‑expanded attribute that mirrors the isOpen state, preventing assistive technology from conveying the menu’s visibility.

issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton used to move to the previous testimonial slide contains only an icon and has no aria‑label, making it inaccessible to screen‑reader users.

issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton used to move to the next testimonial slide contains only an icon and has no aria‑label, preventing screen‑reader users from activating it.

issuesFound.explanation: The two buttons that switch billing period (monthly / annual) act as a toggle group but do not expose their selected state via aria‑pressed, so assistive technology cannot convey which option is active.

issuesFound.title: Filter tab buttons missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The buttons that filter the product catalogue act as a toggle group but lack aria‑pressed, so the selected filter is not announced to screen‑reader users.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form missing aria‑invalid on error state
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput used for the email address receives an “invalid” prop when there is an error, but the TextInput component does not forward this as aria‑invalid, so the error state is not announced.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form broken aria‑describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The email TextInput sets aria‑describedby="newsletter-email-error" when an error exists, but no element with that id is rendered, resulting in a broken reference.
issuesFound.fix: Add an ErrorMessage component with id="newsletter-email-error" inside the FormGroup, or change the aria‑describedby to reference the existing error element.

issuesFound.title: Contact form missing aria‑invalid on error state
issuesFound.explanation: All TextInput and Textarea components receive an “invalid” prop when validation fails, but the underlying components do not output aria‑invalid, so screen readers cannot detect the error state.

issuesFound.title: Contact form broken aria‑describedby references
issuesFound.explanation: Each input’s aria‑describedby points to IDs like "contact-name-error", "contact-email-error", etc., but no elements with those IDs are rendered, creating broken references.
issuesFound.fix: Render corresponding ErrorMessage components with matching IDs inside each FormGroup, or adjust aria‑describedby to reference the existing error message IDs.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative such as a border or box-shadow. This removes the keyboard focus ring for all elements that do not have a more specific focus rule, making keyboard navigation impossible to track.
issuesFound.fix: Line 433: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the @media (forced-colors: active) block, :focus-visible sets outline: none. In Windows High Contrast Mode, box-shadows are suppressed, so removing the outline ensures the focus ring is completely invisible for users relying on this mode.
issuesFound.fix: Line 445: @media (forced-colors: active) { :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid CanvasText; outline-offset: 3px; } }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none and outline-offset: 3px. An offset applied to a zero-width outline renders nothing. There is no box-shadow or border in this rule to indicate focus, violating WCAG 2.4.7.
issuesFound.fix: Line 526: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only an outline-offset. This removes the visible focus ring for the mobile navigation toggle button, hindering keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 623: .nav-toggle:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on submenu links
issuesFound.explanation: The selector [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible sets outline: none without a visible replacement. Dropdown menu links will not show a focus ring when navigated via keyboard.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator relies on box-shadow on #search-input:focus
issuesFound.explanation: The #search-input:focus rule sets outline: none and relies solely on box-shadow for the focus ring. Box-shadows are not visible in Windows High Contrast Mode, making the focus state invisible for users with that setting enabled.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The #search-input:focus-visible selector sets outline: none and outline-offset: 0. This removes the focus ring specifically for keyboard focus on the search input.
issuesFound.fix: Line 697: #search-input:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on search button

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative like a solid outline or border. outline-offset alone is not visible. This removes the keyboard focus ring, making it impossible for keyboard users to see which element is focused.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: Inside @media (forced-colors: active), :focus-visible sets outline: none. This ensures the focus ring is invisible for users using Windows High Contrast Mode, blocking access entirely.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and clip: auto. This fails to hide content visually while keeping it accessible; instead, it renders content normally, cluttering the visual interface, or fails to hide it at all depending on other styles. It does not implement the standard visually-hidden technique.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em. Negative word-spacing reduces the space between words, making text difficult to read for users with cognitive disabilities or low vision, and can cause words to merge.
issuesFound.fix: Line 253: word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets letter-spacing: -0.05em. Negative letter-spacing squeezes characters together, reducing legibility for users with low vision or dyslexia.
issuesFound.fix: Line 254: letter-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class sets min-height: 32px. This is below the recommended 44px minimum touch target size (WCAG 2.2 SC 2.5.8), making it difficult for users with motor impairments to activate on touch screens.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: .btn:focus-visible sets outline: none and only uses outline-offset. Without a solid outline or border, the focus ring is invisible, preventing keyboard users from identifying the focused button.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule that disables this transform for .btn, which may cause discomfort for users sensitive to motion.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 428: (fix requires changes in external stylesheet/script)
  Add to @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce): .btn { transition: none; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px. This is significantly below the 24px absolute minimum and 44px recommended size, making it very hard to tap accurately.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px. This is below the 24px absolute minimum, posing a barrier for users with motor impairments.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: This selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset. The focus ring is removed, hiding keyboard focus state on the logo link.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .nav-toggle
issuesFound.explanation: .nav-toggle sets min-height: 28px and min-width: 28px. This is below the 44px recommended size for touch targets.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: .nav-toggle:focus-visible sets outline: none without a visible replacement. Keyboard users cannot see when the mobile menu toggle is focused.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #nav-menu > li > a, #nav-menu > li > button
issuesFound.explanation: These navigation links/buttons have min-height: 28px, which is below the 44px recommended touch target size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: [id$="-submenu"] li a
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu links have min-height: 28px, below the 44px recommended size.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Focus outline is removed with only offset, making keyboard navigation in dropdowns invisible.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #search-input
issuesFound.explanation: The search input has height: 28px, which is below the 44px recommended touch target size.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus
issuesFound.explanation: #search-input:focus sets outline: none and relies on box-shadow. Box-shadows are not visible in Windows High Contrast Mode, removing the focus indicator for those users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: #search-input:focus-visible sets outline: none with no visible replacement.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button is 28px x 28px, below the 44px recommended size.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Focus outline is removed without a visible alternative, hiding focus state on the search button.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links lose their focus outline, hindering keyboard navigation.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .required-note
issuesFound.explanation: .required-note uses --colour-text-secondary (#aaaaaa) on --colour-bg-page (#ffffff). The contrast ratio is approximately 2.8:1, which is below the 4.5:1 minimum for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability: .section-header h2
issuesFound.explanation: This heading uses var(--letter-spacing-tight) which resolves to -0.025em. Negative letter-spacing reduces legibility.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .section-header p
issuesFound.explanation: This paragraph uses --colour-text-secondary (#aaaaaa) on white background. Contrast is ~2.8:1, failing WCAG AA.

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability: .hero-content h1
issuesFound.explanation: The main heading uses var(--letter-spacing-tighter) (-0.05em), reducing readability.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: .skip-link uses transform: translateY(-200%) with a transition. The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block is empty and does not disable this motion.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 383: (fix requires changes in external stylesheet/script)
  Add to @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce): .skip-link { transition: none; transform: none; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: .hero-image img uses transform with transition. The prefers-reduced-motion override for this selector exists but is empty {}, failing to disable the motion.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 723: (fix requires changes in external stylesheet/script)
  Update @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) to include: .hero-image img { transform: none; transition: none; }

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation overlay visibility not synced with aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation overlay element (_overlay) has its visibility toggled using the hidden attribute in the open and close functions, but the aria-hidden attribute is never updated. This means screen readers may still perceive the overlay as present even when it is visually hidden.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1650: _overlay.removeAttribute('hidden'); _overlay.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'false');
  Line 1663: _overlay.setAttribute('hidden', ''); _overlay.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');

issuesFound.title: Sticky header visibility not synced with aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The sticky header element (_header) has its visibility toggled by adding/removing the headerHidden CSS class, but the aria-hidden attribute is never updated. When the header is visually hidden, it remains in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2005: addClass(_header, CSS.headerHidden); _header.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');
  Line 2015: removeClass(_header, CSS.headerHidden); _header.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panel visibility not synced with aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu panel (inst.panel) has its visibility toggled using the hidden attribute in _openInstance and _closeInstance, but the aria-hidden attribute is never updated. This causes a mismatch between visual and accessibility states.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1795: inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden'); inst.panel.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'false');
  Line 1815: inst.panel.setAttribute('hidden', ''); inst.panel.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');

issuesFound.title: Search suggestion listbox visibility not synced with aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The search suggestion listbox (_listEl) has its visibility toggled using the hidden attribute in show and hide functions, but the aria-hidden attribute is never updated. Screen readers may announce the listbox even when it is visually hidden.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2295: _listEl.removeAttribute('hidden'); _listEl.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'false');
  Line 2305: _listEl.setAttribute('hidden', ''); _listEl.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panel visibility not synced with aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ answer panel (item.panel) has its visibility toggled using the hidden attribute in open and close functions, but the aria-hidden attribute is never updated. This leaves the content accessible to screen readers when it should be hidden.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2695: item.panel.removeAttribute('hidden'); item.panel.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'false');
  Line 2715: item.panel.setAttribute('hidden', ''); item.panel.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');

issuesFound.title: Active filter chips container visibility not synced with aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The active filter chips container (_containerEl) has its visibility toggled using the hidden attribute in the render function, but the aria-hidden attribute is never updated. This creates a discrepancy for assistive technology users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2490: _containerEl.removeAttribute('hidden'); _containerEl.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'false');
  Line 2495: _containerEl.setAttribute('hidden', ''); _containerEl.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');

issuesFound.title: Pricing comparison table visibility not synced with aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing comparison table has its visibility toggled using the hidden attribute in setComparisonTableExpanded, but the aria-hidden attribute is never updated. The table remains in the accessibility tree when visually hidden.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2615: table.removeAttribute('hidden'); table.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'false');
  Line 2620: table.setAttribute('hidden', ''); table.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');

issuesFound.title: Discount code status message remains hidden after update
issuesFound.explanation: The discount code status element (_statusEl) is initialized with the hidden attribute (Line 2695), but the _onApply function (Line 2675) updates the text content without removing the hidden attribute. The status message is never visible to any user.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2675: _statusEl.textContent = result.message; _statusEl.removeAttribute('hidden');

issuesFound.title: Toast dismissal does not set aria-hidden before removal
issuesFound.explanation: The toast dismissal function removes CSS classes to fade out the toast but does not set aria-hidden="true" before the element is removed from the DOM. If the fade transition takes time, the toast remains accessible during the animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1315: removeClass(t, CSS.TOAST_SHOW); addClass(t, CSS.TOAST_HIDE); t.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu state changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance and _closeInstance functions in dropdownMenu update the visual state and ARIA expanded attribute but do not announce the state change (e.g., "Menu opened") to screen readers via a live region.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1795: _openInstance: ... liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');
  Line 1808: _closeInstance: ... liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');

issuesFound.title: Cookie consent decision not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The _onAccept and _onDecline functions in cookieConsent update analytics and hide the banner but do not announce the user's decision (e.g., "Cookies accepted") to screen readers via a live region.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 4315: _onAccept: ... liveRegion.announce('Cookies accepted');
  Line 4325: _onDecline: ... liveRegion.announce('Cookies declined');

issuesFound.title: Discount code input aria-invalid not set on validation error
issuesFound.explanation: The _onApply function in discountCodeInput validates the input and displays an error message but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the input element (_inputEl) when the code is invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2675: if (!result.valid) { _inputEl.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); } else { _inputEl.removeAttribute('aria-invalid'); }

issuesFound.title: themeSwitcher module not initialized in app bootstrap
issuesFound.explanation: The themeSwitcher module is defined with an init function (Line 4545) but is not called in the app.init bootstrap sequence (Line 4735). Theme toggle buttons will not function or update ARIA states.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4735: _safeInit('themeSwitcher', () => themeSwitcher.init());

issuesFound.title: mediaPlayerController module not initialized in app bootstrap
issuesFound.explanation: The mediaPlayerController module is defined with an init function (Line 4495) but is not called in the app.init bootstrap sequence (Line 4735). Media elements will not receive keyboard accessibility enhancements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4735: _safeInit('mediaPlayerController', () => mediaPlayerController.init());
