issuesFound.title: statCounterAnimator dynamic content not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The _animate function updates the textContent of stat elements to show counting numbers. While it sets aria-label on completion, it does not use liveRegion.announce to notify screen reader users of the change if they are not focused on that specific element. This violates WCAG 4.1.3 (Status Messages).
issuesFound.fix: Line 4475: liveRegion.announce(`${cfg.prefix}${cfg.target}${cfg.suffix}`);

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent accept/decline actions not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The _onAccept and _onDecline functions hide the banner and store preferences but do not announce the result (e.g., "Preferences saved") to screen readers. Users may not know their action was successful.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 4505: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.cookieSaved || 'Cookie preferences saved.');
  Line 4515: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.cookieSaved || 'Cookie preferences saved.');

issuesFound.title: discountCodeInput validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The _onApply function displays an error message in _statusEl when a discount code is invalid, but it does not set aria-invalid="true" on the _inputEl. Screen readers may not associate the error with the input field.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3335: if (_inputEl) _inputEl.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: fileUploadField validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The _addFile function announces validation errors via liveRegion but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the binding.inputEl. This disconnects the error state from the form control for assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4215: binding.inputEl.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: themeSwitcher interactive elements missing keyboard support
issuesFound.explanation: The init function attaches a click listener to [data-theme-toggle] elements. If these elements are not native buttons or links, they will not be activatable via keyboard (Enter/Space), violating WCAG 2.1.1 (Keyboard).
issuesFound.fix: Line 4755: document.addEventListener('keydown', function _onThemeKeydown(ev) { const btn = closest(ev.target, '[data-theme-toggle]'); if (!btn) return; if (ev.key === KEYS.ENTER || ev.key === KEYS.SPACE) { ev.preventDefault(); btn.click(); } });

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop button missing keyboard support
issuesFound.explanation: The init function attaches a click listener to _btn. If _btn is not a native button or link, it lacks keyboard activation support (Enter/Space), violating WCAG 2.1.1 (Keyboard).
issuesFound.fix: Line 1990: _btn.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => { if (e.key === KEYS.ENTER || e.key === KEYS.SPACE) { e.preventDefault(); _onClick(); } });

issuesFound.title: productFilter view toggle elements missing keyboard support
issuesFound.explanation: The init function uses delegate to attach a click listener to [data-view-mode] elements. If these are not native buttons, they cannot be activated via keyboard, violating WCAG 2.1.1 (Keyboard).
issuesFound.fix: Line 2545: delegate(document.body, '[data-view-mode]', 'keydown', (e, btn) => { if (e.key === KEYS.ENTER || e.key === KEYS.SPACE) { e.preventDefault(); btn.click(); } });

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel control buttons missing keyboard support enforcement
issuesFound.explanation: The init function attaches click listeners to _playBtn, _prevBtn, and _nextBtn. It does not verify they are native buttons or add role/tabindex/keydown handlers if they are not, risking keyboard inaccessibility.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3645: [_playBtn, _prevBtn, _nextBtn].forEach(btn => { if (btn && btn.tagName !== 'BUTTON') { btn.setAttribute('role', 'button'); btn.setAttribute('tabindex', '0'); btn.addEventListener('keydown', e => { if (e.key === KEYS.ENTER || e.key === KEYS.SPACE) { e.preventDefault(); btn.click(); } }); } });

issuesFound.title: multiStepWizard navigation buttons missing keyboard support enforcement
issuesFound.explanation: The init function attaches click listeners to _prevBtn, _nextBtn, and _submitBtn. It does not ensure they are keyboard accessible if they are not native buttons, violating WCAG 2.1.1 (Keyboard).
issuesFound.fix: Line 4055: [_prevBtn, _nextBtn, _submitBtn].forEach(btn => { if (btn && btn.tagName !== 'BUTTON') { btn.setAttribute('role', 'button'); btn.setAttribute('tabindex', '0'); btn.addEventListener('keydown', e => { if (e.key === KEYS.ENTER || e.key === KEYS.SPACE) { e.preventDefault(); btn.click(); } }); } });

issuesFound.title: siteSearch clear button missing keyboard support enforcement
issuesFound.explanation: The init function attaches a click listener to _clearBtn. It does not ensure the element is keyboard accessible if it is not a native button, violating WCAG 2.1.1 (Keyboard).
issuesFound.fix: Line 2200: if (_clearBtn && _clearBtn.tagName !== 'BUTTON') { _clearBtn.setAttribute('role', 'button'); _clearBtn.setAttribute('tabindex', '0'); _clearBtn.addEventListener('keydown', e => { if (e.key === KEYS.ENTER || e.key === KEYS.SPACE) { e.preventDefault(); _onClearClick(e); } }); }

issuesFound.title: discountCodeInput apply button missing keyboard support enforcement
issuesFound.explanation: The init function attaches a click listener to _applyBtn. It does not ensure the element is keyboard accessible if it is not a native button, violating WCAG 2.1.1 (Keyboard).
issuesFound.fix: Line 3315: if (_applyBtn && _applyBtn.tagName !== 'BUTTON') { _applyBtn.setAttribute('role', 'button'); _applyBtn.setAttribute('tabindex', '0'); _applyBtn.addEventListener('keydown', e => { if (e.key === KEYS.ENTER || e.key === KEYS.SPACE) { e.preventDefault(); _onApply(); } }); }

issuesFound.title: stickyHeader visually hides header without updating aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The _onScroll function within stickyHeader adds the headerHidden class to visually hide the header element when scrolling down, but it does not set aria-hidden="true" on the header. This leaves the header accessible to screen readers even when it is not visible, causing confusion for users navigating by landmarks.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1674: addClass(_header, CSS.headerHidden); _header.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');
  Line 1681: removeClass(_header, CSS.headerHidden); _header.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'false');

issuesFound.title: discountCodeInput validation error does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The _onApply function in discountCodeInput displays an error message in _statusEl when a discount code is invalid, but it fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the _inputEl. Screen reader users are not informed that the input field itself is in an error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2972: setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: fileUploadField validation error does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The _addFile function in fileUploadField announces an error via liveRegion when a file is invalid (size/type), but it does not set aria-invalid="true" on the inputEl or zoneEl. The input field is not programmatically marked as invalid for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3365: setAttr(binding.inputEl, 'aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent banner appearance not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The _showBanner function in cookieConsent dynamically injects or reveals the consent banner and sets aria-hidden="false", but it does not use liveRegion.announce to inform screen reader users that the banner has appeared. Users may be unaware of the new dialog.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3678: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.cookieBannerShown || 'Cookie preferences banner opened');

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent accept/decline action result not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The _onAccept and _onDecline functions in cookieConsent hide the banner upon user action but do not announce a confirmation message (e.g., "Preferences saved"). Users are not informed that their choice was successfully recorded.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3636: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.cookiePreferencesSaved || 'Cookie preferences saved');
  Line 3642: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.cookiePreferencesSaved || 'Cookie preferences saved');

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel play button aria-pressed not initialised if autoplay disabled
issuesFound.explanation: The init function in testimonialsCarousel only calls play() (which sets aria-pressed) if autoplay is enabled. If autoplay is disabled, the _playBtn's aria-pressed attribute is never initialised, leaving its state unknown to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2775: if (!_playing && _playBtn) { setAttr(_playBtn, 'aria-pressed', 'false'); }

issuesFound.title: statCounterAnimator dynamic number updates not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The _animate function in statCounterAnimator updates the textContent of the element with changing numbers, but it does not announce the final value or the update via a live region. Screen reader users may miss the updated statistic entirely.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3565: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.statUpdated || 'Statistic updated to ' + _format(cfg.target, cfg));

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The open function adds the is-open class and removes hidden from the drawer but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the _trigger button. The close function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", creating an asymmetric state where screen readers are not informed when the menu opens.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance function removes the hidden attribute and adds the open class to the panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The paired _closeInstance function sets aria-expanded="false", leaving the expanded state out of sync with the visual state.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The open function sets item.open = true and removes hidden from the panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on item.trigger. The close function sets aria-expanded="false", causing screen readers to announce the wrong state when the answer expands.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close function does not announce state change to live region
issuesFound.explanation: The close function emits a custom event but does not call liveRegion.announce to inform screen reader users that the navigation menu has closed. The open function correctly announces "Navigation menu opened", but the closing action is silent.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.close function does not announce state change to live region
issuesFound.explanation: The close function emits a custom event but does not call liveRegion.announce to inform screen reader users that the FAQ answer has collapsed. The open function correctly announces the expansion, but the collapse is not announced.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance and _closeInstance do not announce state changes
issuesFound.explanation: Neither the _openInstance nor _closeInstance functions call liveRegion.announce to inform screen reader users when a dropdown menu opens or closes. Users are not notified of the visibility change of the menu panel.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav overlay visibility not synced with aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The open and close functions toggle the hidden attribute on the _overlay element but do not toggle aria-hidden. This leaves the overlay in the accessibility tree when visually hidden or removes it when visible, depending on browser handling of hidden vs aria-hidden.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu panel visibility not synced with aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance and _closeInstance functions toggle the hidden attribute on inst.panel but do not toggle aria-hidden. This fails to properly remove the panel from the accessibility tree when closed.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion panel visibility not synced with aria-hidden

issuesFound.title: discountCodeInput._onApply does not set aria-invalid on input

issuesFound.title: fileUploadField._validateFile does not set aria-invalid on input
issuesFound.explanation: The _validateFile function returns a validation result and the caller announces the error via live region, but the input element (binding.inputEl) is never updated with aria-invalid="true". The input's invalid state is not reflected in the accessibility tree.

issuesFound.title: multiStepWizard._goToStep may lose focus if no heading exists

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid

This prevents assistive technologies from identifying the field as invalid. This affects all fields using inlineValidator (e.g., #newsletter-email, [name="full_name"], [name="email"]).

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid

issuesFound.fix: Line: srUtils.markValid function: add field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false'); before removing aria-describedby.

issuesFound.fix: Line 615: (add after transition block) Line 470: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The skip link uses a transform transition for its slide-in animation without a prefers-reduced-motion override, which can cause issues for users sensitive to motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 449: (add to existing media query at line 470) Line 470: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: The hero CTA button includes transform in its transition for hover effects but lacks a reduced-motion override, potentially affecting users with motion sensitivity.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1069: (add to existing media query at line 470) Line 470: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on global :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The selector :focus-visible sets outline: none without providing a visible replacement such as box-shadow or border in the same rule-block. This removes the focus ring for all interactive elements in default mode, making keyboard navigation invisible. The forced-colors media query provides an outline for high-contrast mode, but no equivalent replacement exists for standard mode.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient focus indicator on .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The selector .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible only adjusts outline-offset to -3px without setting an outline width or style. Since the global :focus-visible rule sets outline: none, this results in no visible focus ring for elements using this class.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient focus indicator on .focus-ring-brand:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The selector .focus-ring-brand:focus-visible only sets outline-color without specifying outline-width or outline-style. With the global outline: none rule, this produces no visible focus ring.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class sets min-height: 32px, which is below the 44px recommended target size (WCAG 2.5.8). While this meets the absolute minimum of 24px, it may still be difficult for users with motor impairments to activate reliably.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 36px, which is below the 44px recommended target size. This reduced size may cause difficulty for users with tremors or limited dexterity.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The selector #site-search button[type="submit"] sets width: 28px and height: 28px, which is below the 44px recommended size and only marginally above the 24px minimum. This small touch target may be difficult to activate accurately.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on checkbox inputs
issuesFound.explanation: The selector .form-group input[type="checkbox"] sets width: 20px, height: 20px, and min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8. This makes the checkbox difficult to activate for users with motor impairments.

issuesFound.title: Touch target at minimum size on footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The selector footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a sets min-height: 24px, which meets the absolute minimum but falls below the 44px recommended size for comfortable touch activation.

issuesFound.title: Broken visually-hidden utility class removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and overflow: visible, which keeps the content in the document flow and visible. This fails to hide the content visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers. The class should use position: absolute with 1px dimensions and clipping.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property (transition: ... transform var(--transition-fast)), which causes spatial movement during state changes. No @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override exists to disable this transform for users sensitive to motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero CTA button
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-ctas .btn-primary selector includes transform in its transition, creating spatial movement on hover/focus. No reduced-motion override is provided for this specific transition.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (forced-colors: active) block removes the outline entirely (outline: none) and provides only outline-offset. In Windows High Contrast Mode, box-shadow is suppressed and outline is the only reliable focus indicator; removing it leaves users with no visible focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: This utility class inherits outline: none from the base :focus-visible rule and only adjusts outline-offset. Without an outline-style and width, no focus ring is rendered even when the class is applied.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-brand:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: This utility class sets outline-color but inherits outline: none from the base :focus-visible rule. Setting only the color does not restore the outline; a visible ring requires outline-style and width.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The button focus state sets outline: none and only provides outline-offset. With no box-shadow, border, or non-zero outline in the same rule, keyboard users cannot see when buttons receive focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on submenu links
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu links ([id$="-submenu"] li a) have outline: none with only outline-offset in their focus-visible state. No border or box-shadow replacement is provided, hiding focus from keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The search form submit button sets outline: none with only outline-offset, leaving no visible focus indicator for keyboard navigation.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on account navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links set outline: none with only outline-offset. No alternative visual focus indicator is provided in the rule.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and clip: auto, which fails to visually hide content. Screen-reader-only text becomes visible in the layout, and the incorrect properties may remove content from the accessibility tree or cause layout disruption.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The skip-link component uses transform: translateY(-200%) and transition: transform, creating spatial movement. Users who prefer reduced motion have no mechanism to disable this animation.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero primary button
issuesFound.explanation: The hero primary button defines a transition including transform, creating spatial movement on hover/focus. No reduced-motion override is provided for users sensitive to motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero image
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image uses transition: transform with perspective transforms, creating spatial movement animations. The existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 903 is empty and fails to disable the animation.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8, making it difficult for users with motor impairments to activate.
issuesFound.fix: Line 541: `.btn-sm { min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-1-5) var(--space-3-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, below the 24px minimum touch target size, creating a barrier for users with tremors or limited dexterity.
issuesFound.fix: Line 553: `.btn-icon { min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-2-5); border-radius: var(--radius-full); aspect-ratio: 1; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on search input
issuesFound.explanation: #search-input sets height: 28px, which is below the 44px recommended touch target size and may be difficult to tap accurately on touch devices.
issuesFound.fix: Line 596: `#search-input { width: 220px; height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-3); padding-right: var(--space-10); ... }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on search button
issuesFound.explanation: #site-search button[type="submit"] sets width: 28px and height: 28px, below the 44px recommended size without sufficient padding compensation in the rule.
issuesFound.fix: Line 615: `#site-search button[type="submit"] { position: absolute; right: var(--space-3); display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 44px; height: 44px; ... }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a sets min-height: 24px, which meets the absolute minimum but is below the 44px recommended size without adequate padding compensation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1073: `footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-400); text-decoration: none; transition: color var(--transition-fast); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); display: inline-block; min-height: 44px; }`

issuesFound.title: Broken visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class incorrectly uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and overflow: visible, which makes the content visible on screen rather than hiding it visually while keeping it accessible to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 469-480: `.sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation without prefers-reduced-motion override on buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 461 is empty and does not disable this animation for users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Line 461-463: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation without prefers-reduced-motion override on hero image
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img class includes transform in its transition, but the corresponding @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 720 is empty.
issuesFound.fix: Line 720-723: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces space between words and can impair readability for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 318: `body { ... word-spacing: normal; ... }`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on blockquote text
issuesFound.explanation: The blockquote selector sets color: var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) against the default white background, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.3:1, which fails the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 414: `blockquote { border-left: 4px solid var(--colour-border-brand); margin: 0; padding-left: var(--space-6); font-style: italic; color: var(--colour-neutral-700); }`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer bottom text
issuesFound.explanation: The .footer-bottom > p selector sets color: var(--colour-neutral-500) (#64748b) against background-color: var(--colour-neutral-900) (#0f172a), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 3.85:1, which fails the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text (12px font size).
issuesFound.fix: Line 1079: `.footer-bottom > p { font-size: var(--font-size-xs); color: var(--colour-neutral-400); margin: 0; line-height: var(--line-height-normal); }`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none without providing an alternative non-colour visual differentiator (such as border-bottom or increased font-weight) for links in their default state, causing links to be distinguished from surrounding text by colour alone.
issuesFound.fix: Line 300: `a { text-decoration: underline; }` or add `border-bottom: 1px solid currentColor;` to provide a non-colour indicator.

issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible pseudo-class sets outline: none and only provides outline-offset: 3px without a visible outline, box-shadow, or border. This removes the focus indicator entirely for keyboard users, making it impossible to see which element has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 348: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.explanation: Inside the forced-colors media query, the :focus-visible rule sets outline: none with only outline-offset, providing no visible focus indicator for high-contrast mode users who rely on focus outlines.

issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class incorrectly uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and overflow: visible, which makes the content visible to sighted users instead of hiding it visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers. issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on featured pricing card
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-card--featured:hover selector applies a transform animation (translateY and scale) that is not covered by the existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block, which only targets .pricing-card:hover. Users who prefer reduced motion will still see the animation on featured cards.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2054: (add to existing @media block at line 2037) .pricing-card--featured:hover { transform: none; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and only provides outline-offset, which does not create a visible focus ring. Keyboard users cannot see which element has focus. The outline-offset property alone does not render any visible indicator when the outline width is zero.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus:not(:focus-visible)
issuesFound.explanation: The selector removes the outline for focused elements that are not focus-visible (mouse focus) without providing any visible alternative. While this targets mouse interactions, the rule removes the default focus indicator entirely without replacement, violating the requirement that focus indicators must be visible.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on forced-colors :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the forced-colors media query, the :focus-visible rule sets outline: none with only outline-offset, removing the focus indicator in high-contrast mode where box-shadow alternatives are suppressed. Users in forced-colors mode cannot see focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The selector only sets outline-offset: -3px but does not define an outline width, style, or color. It relies on the base :focus-visible rule which sets outline: none, resulting in no visible focus indicator for elements using this class.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu link focus state sets outline: none and only provides outline-offset: -3px. Without a visible outline width and color, keyboard users cannot see which menu item is focused.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search input focus-visible state sets outline: none with outline-offset: 0, removing the focus ring without providing a visible alternative. Although the :focus state has a box-shadow, the :focus-visible state explicitly removes the outline without replacement.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button focus-visible state sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px. This does not create a visible focus ring, making it impossible for keyboard users to see when the button is focused.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The form input focus-visible state sets outline: none with outline-offset: 0. While the :focus state has a box-shadow, the :focus-visible state removes the outline without providing a visible alternative that works in forced-colors mode.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group select:focus-visible

issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox focus-visible state sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px. This does not create a visible focus ring around the checkbox, making it difficult for keyboard users to see when it is focused.

issuesFound.explanation: The icon button sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, below the 24px minimum. This creates a touch target that is too small for reliable activation by users with motor disabilities.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input sets width: 20px, height: 20px, and min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size. This makes it difficult for users with motor impairments to toggle the checkbox.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The base button class sets min-height: 32px. While this meets the 24px minimum, it is below the 44px recommended target size for enhanced accessibility. Users with motor impairments may find this button difficult to activate reliably.

issuesFound.explanation: The large button variant sets min-height: 36px. While this meets the 24px minimum, it is below the 44px recommended target size for optimal accessibility.

issuesFound.explanation: The hero CTA primary button sets min-height: 32px. While this meets the 24px minimum, it is below the 44px recommended target size for reliable activation.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on .hero-ctas .btn-secondary

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on .product-body .btn-secondary

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and overflow: visible, which makes the content visible to sighted users and does not hide it from screen readers properly. More critically, it fails to use the clip technique and uses properties that prevent the content from being hidden visually. This breaks the accessibility utility entirely, causing screen-reader-only text to be visible to all users or potentially hidden incorrectly.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The base button class includes transform in its transition property (transition: transform var(--transition-fast)), enabling spatial movement animations. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this transform transition for users who prefer reduced motion, which can trigger vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: The hero CTA button includes transform in its transition and uses transform: translateY(-1px) on hover. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this spatial movement for users sensitive to motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image includes transition: transform var(--transition-slower) and uses transform: perspective(...) rotateY(...) rotateX(...). The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 773-778 is empty and does not override or disable these transformations, violating the requirement to respect user motion preferences.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which is a negative value. This reduces the space between words, making it difficult for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities to distinguish between words and read the content.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .btn-danger
issuesFound.explanation: The danger button uses color: var(--colour-neutral-0) (#ffffff) on background-color: var(--colour-error) (#ee9999). The contrast ratio is approximately 1.5:1, which fails WCAG AA requirements (4.5:1 for normal text). Users with low vision cannot read the white text on the light red background.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu controller opens and closes dropdown panels via _openInstance and _closeInstance functions that update aria-expanded and CSS classes, but neither function writes to an aria-live region. Screen reader users receive no confirmation that the menu has opened or closed, particularly problematic when the menu content loads asynchronously or when focus does not immediately move into the panel.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1310: Add `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');` after `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`. Line 1326: Add `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');` after `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Discount code input validation lacks aria-invalid attribution
issuesFound.explanation: In discountCodeInput._onApply, when validation fails, the error message is written to _statusEl but the input field (_inputEl) does not receive aria-invalid="true". Screen reader users navigating back to the field will not hear that it is in an error state, violating SC 3.3.1 and 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2280: After `_statusEl.textContent = result.message || '';`, add `srUtils.markInvalid(_inputEl, _statusEl);`.

issuesFound.title: Sticky header visually hidden but remains in accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The stickyHeader.hide() and show() functions toggle CSS classes to slide the header out of view, but they do not set aria-hidden="true" on the header element when hidden. Depending on the CSS implementation (transform: translateY), the header may remain focusable and navigable by screen readers even when visually hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1420: Add `setAttr(_header, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` in the hide() function. Line 1445: Add `setAttr(_header, 'aria-hidden', 'false');` in the show() function.

issuesFound.title: Stat counter animation completion not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The statCounterAnimator._animate function animates numeric values but only updates the DOM textContent. When the animation completes, the final statistic is not announced via the live region system, meaning screen reader users who navigate past the element during or after animation may not perceive the updated value.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3350: In the onComplete callback of animateNumber, add `liveRegion.announce('Statistic updated: ' + el.textContent);`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing comparison table expansion not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setComparisonTableExpanded function toggles the visibility of the comparison table and updates aria-expanded on the trigger, but does not announce the state change via a live region. Users toggling the table do not receive confirmation that the content has expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2260: Add `liveRegion.announce(expanded ? 'Comparison table expanded' : 'Comparison table collapsed');` after `setExpanded(trigger, expanded);`.

issuesFound.title: Wizard navigation buttons lack aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: In multiStepWizard.init, the previous and next buttons (_prevBtn, _nextBtn) control the visibility of step panels but do not have aria-controls attributes referencing the current active panel ID. This prevents screen reader users from understanding the relationship between the navigation buttons and the content they control.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2800: After selecting _prevBtn and _nextBtn, add `setAttr(_prevBtn, 'aria-controls', _steps[_current].panelEl.id);` and `setAttr(_nextBtn, 'aria-controls', _steps[_current].panelEl.id);`, updating these attributes in the _goToStep function as well.

issuesFound.title: FAQ search term highlighting not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.highlightFaqTerms function wraps search matches in <mark> elements for visual highlighting but does not announce the number of matches found or which terms were highlighted. Screen reader users receive no feedback that the search has filtered or highlighted content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2490: After highlighting, add `liveRegion.announce(`${tokens.length} matches found for "${query}"`);`.

issuesFound.title: Form autosave not announced to users
issuesFound.explanation: The formAutosave.save function persists form data to localStorage silently. Users are not informed that their progress has been auto-saved, which may cause anxiety about data loss or uncertainty about whether they need to manually save.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3100: Add `liveRegion.announce('Form progress auto-saved');` at the end of the save function.

issuesFound.title: Active filter chip removal not specifically announced
issuesFound.explanation: When a user removes a filter chip in activeFilterChips._onChipClick, the product grid updates and announces the new count, but the specific action of removing the filter (e.g., "Removed category: Design") is not announced, potentially confusing users about which filter was just removed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1950: Add `liveRegion.announce(`Removed filter: ${label}`);` before calling the productFilter update functions.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer lacks aria-modal attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function activates a focus trap on the navigation drawer, indicating it behaves as a modal dialog, but it does not set aria-modal="true" on the drawer element (_drawer). Screen readers may not convey the modal context to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1150: Add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-modal', 'true');` in the open() function.

issuesFound.title: Carousel navigation buttons lack accessible name initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The testimonialsCarousel.init function selects previous and next buttons via data attributes but does not ensure they have accessible names (aria-label) if the HTML lacks text content. Unlabeled buttons violate SC 2.4.4 and 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2650: After selecting _prevBtn and _nextBtn, add `if (!_prevBtn.getAttribute('aria-label')) setAttr(_prevBtn, 'aria-label', 'Previous testimonial');` and `if (!_nextBtn.getAttribute('aria-label')) setAttr(_nextBtn, 'aria-label', 'Next testimonial');`.

issuesFound.title: Scroll to top button lacks accessible name initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The scrollToTop.init function selects the scroll-to-top button but does not verify or set an accessible name (aria-label). If the HTML button lacks text or an aria-label, screen readers will announce it as "button" without context.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1480: After selecting _btn, add `if (!_btn.getAttribute('aria-label')) setAttr(_btn, 'aria-label', 'Scroll back to top');`.

issuesFound.title: Search clear button lacks accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch.init function selects the clear button (_clearBtn) but does not ensure it has an accessible name. If the HTML element lacks text or aria-label, screen reader users will not understand the button's purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1680: After selecting _clearBtn, add `if (_clearBtn && !_clearBtn.getAttribute('aria-label')) setAttr(_clearBtn, 'aria-label', 'Clear search');`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle lacks accessible name initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init function selects the navigation toggle button (_trigger) and sets aria-expanded and aria-controls, but does not ensure the button has an accessible name (aria-label) if the HTML lacks text content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1120: After selecting _trigger, add `if (!_trigger.getAttribute('aria-label')) setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-label', 'Toggle navigation menu');`.

issuesFound.title: Wizard step panels lack aria-labelledby
issuesFound.explanation: In multiStepWizard.init, step panels are assigned role="tabpanel" but do not have aria-labelledby attributes linking them to their respective step title elements (h2, h3, or [data-wizard-focus]). Screen readers cannot convey the purpose of each panel when users navigate to them.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2800: In the _parseSteps function or init loop, add `step.panelEl.setAttribute('aria-labelledby', srUtils.ensureId(qs('h2, h3, [data-step-title]', step.panelEl) || step.panelEl, 'step-title'));`.

issuesFound.title: File upload zone lacks aria-describedby linking to instructions
issuesFound.explanation: The fileUploadField.bind function creates an upload zone with role="button" but does not associate it with instructional text (e.g., file type restrictions, max size) via aria-describedby. Users may not understand the constraints until after attempting to upload.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3120: After creating the zone, add `const descId = srUtils.ensureId(qs('[data-upload-help]', zoneEl) || createElement('span', {id: 'upload-help'}, 'Accepted files: ' + config.allowedMimeTypes.join(', ')), 'upload-help'); setAttr(zoneEl, 'aria-describedby', descId);`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle buttons lack accessible name verification
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.init function discovers period toggle buttons (_periodBtns) but does not ensure they have accessible names. If the HTML uses icons or lacks text, these buttons will be unlabeled.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2200: In the _syncToggleState function or init, add `_periodBtns.forEach(btn => { if (!btn.getAttribute('aria-label')) setAttr(btn, 'aria-label', 'Switch to ' + btn.getAttribute('data-period') + ' billing'); });`.

issuesFound.title: Theme switcher buttons lack accessible name verification
issuesFound.explanation: The themeSwitcher.init function attaches click handlers to theme toggle buttons but does not verify or set aria-label attributes. If the HTML relies on icons without text, the buttons will lack accessible names.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3400: In the click handler or init, add `if (!btn.getAttribute('aria-label')) setAttr(btn, 'aria-label', 'Switch to ' + newTheme + ' theme');`.

issuesFound.title: Cookie consent banner trigger lacks accessible name verification
issuesFound.explanation: The cookieConsent.init function listens for clicks on [data-cookie-prefs] buttons but does not ensure these buttons have accessible names if they rely on icons or visual cues alone.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3500: In the _onPrefsClick handler or init, add `if (!btn.getAttribute('aria-label')) setAttr(btn, 'aria-label', 'Open cookie preferences');`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _onTriggerClick handler lacks live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The click handler on dropdown trigger elements toggles the visible state of the dropdown panel (showing or hiding the menu), but does not write to an aria-live region to announce the state change to screen readers. While focus remains on the trigger button, the appearance/disappearance of the panel is a dynamic content change that should be communicated.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu hover close handler lacks live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When the user moves the mouse away from a dropdown wrapper, a timer closes the dropdown panel after a delay. This visible content change (panel disappearing) occurs without an announcement to screen readers, leaving users unaware that the menu has closed.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav viewport resize close handler lacks live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When the viewport is resized beyond the mobile breakpoint, the navigation drawer automatically closes. This visible content change (drawer closing) is triggered by the resize event (user action) but no aria-live announcement is made to inform screen reader users that the menu has closed.

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod function lacks live region announcement for price values
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is toggled (monthly/annual), the price display elements update their values dynamically. While the function announces the billing period change ("Billing period changed to..."), it does not announce the actual price values that have changed, leaving screen reader users unaware of the new cost information.

issuesFound.title: Toast notification conflicting ARIA live region attributes
issuesFound.explanation: The toast container creates elements with role="alert" (which implies assertive) but then sets aria-live="polite" for non-error toast types. This creates conflicting accessibility semantics that may cause screen readers to announce important notifications with the wrong priority or inconsistently.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1177: Change role to "status" for non-error toasts, or remove aria-live and rely solely on role="alert" for errors and role="status" for other types.

issuesFound.title: Pricing comparison table headers misuse aria-selected
issuesFound.explanation: The planComparisonHighlighter sets aria-selected="true" on table header (<th>) elements when highlighting columns. The aria-selected attribute is not appropriate for table headers; it is intended for gridcell, option, and similar roles. This may cause screen readers to misinterpret the table structure.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3014: Remove the aria-selected attribute from the <th> elements. Use a visually distinct class or aria-current="true" instead to indicate the active column.

issuesFound.title: Testimonial carousel slides missing explicit ARIA role
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel slides are given aria-roledescription="slide" but lack an explicit role attribute (such as role="group" or role="region"). Without an explicit role, screen readers may not correctly interpret the custom roledescription, making the carousel structure unclear.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3052: Add role="group" (or role="region" if titled) to each slide element when setting aria-roledescription, e.g., `el.setAttribute('role', 'group'); el.setAttribute('aria-roledescription', 'slide');`.

issuesFound.title: Multi-step wizard improper tabpanel pattern
issuesFound.explanation: The wizard step panels have role="tabpanel" but the navigation buttons (prev/next) do not have role="tab" and are not contained in a role="tablist". This creates an incomplete tab interface pattern that may confuse screen reader users expecting standard tab behavior.
issuesFound.fix: Either remove role="tabpanel" from the step panels and use aria-labelledby to associate them with step headings, or restructure the navigation to use role="tablist" with role="tab" buttons and proper aria-controls associations.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu hover interactions lack live region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: The _onHover function opens and closes dropdown menus in response to mouse hover but does not announce these state changes via a live region. Since hover does not move keyboard focus, screen reader users navigating with a mouse may not be aware that the menu has opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2340: Add liveRegion.announce calls within _onHover when opening ('Menu opened') and closing ('Menu closed') to inform screen reader users of the visibility change.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu close on outside interaction lacks announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The _onDocumentClick and _onDocumentFocusin functions close all dropdowns when clicking or focusing outside the navigation, but they do not announce this closure. Users may not realize the menu has disappeared, especially if focus remains on the trigger button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2360: Add liveRegion.announce('Menu closed') or similar within _closeAll when invoked from these handlers (excluding cases where focus is explicitly returned to the trigger).

issuesFound.title: Site search suggestion close on outside click lacks announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The _onDocumentClick handler closes the search suggestion panel when clicking outside, but does not announce the closure. Screen reader users may continue to believe the suggestion list is available.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2584: Add liveRegion.announce('Search suggestions closed') within _close() when triggered by outside click (as opposed to selection).

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel autoplay pause on hover lacks announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel pauses automatic rotation when the mouse hovers over it (and resumes on mouseleave), updating the aria-pressed state of the play/pause button. However, this automatic state change is not announced via a live region, so screen reader users are not informed that the carousel has paused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3134: Add liveRegion.announce('Carousel paused') within the pause() function and liveRegion.announce('Carousel playing') within play() when these are triggered by hover/focus events.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation close on viewport resize lacks announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When the viewport is resized above the mobile breakpoint, the mobile navigation drawer is automatically closed via _onBreakpointChange with announceToSR set to false. While this is a system-initiated change, providing an announcement would help users understand why the navigation disappeared.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2260: Change `close(false)` to `close(true)` or explicitly announce 'Navigation closed' when auto-closing due to breakpoint change.

issuesFound.title: Active filter chip removal causes focus loss without announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When a filter chip is clicked to remove a filter, the chip button is removed from the DOM. Focus is not programmatically moved to a logical location (such as the next chip or the filter container), and while the filter change is announced, the focus loss may disorient keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2905: After removing the chip, move focus to the chip container or the previous/next chip. For example: `const nextChip = _containerEl.querySelector('button'); if (nextChip) nextChip.focus();`.

issuesFound.title: File upload zone lacks keyboard activation announcement
issuesFound.explanation: While the upload zone handles Enter and Space keys to activate the file input, it does not announce when the file dialog opens. Although the OS file dialog is modal, providing confirmation that the action was triggered can improve user confidence.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3645: Add liveRegion.announce('File dialog opened') after triggering `inputEl.click()` in the keydown handler.

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle switch lacks role announcement on initialization
issuesFound.explanation: When the pricing toggle is not a native <button>, the code sets role="switch" and tabindex="0", but it does not ensure that the element is explicitly announced as a switch on first focus. Additionally, the aria-checked value is set, but the initial announcement might be missed if the element is not focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2956: Ensure the toggle element has a descriptive aria-label or aria-labelledby, and verify that aria-checked is set before the element receives focus. No code change needed if HTML provides label, but verify `aria-label` is set dynamically if not present in HTML.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() missing aria-invalid attribute

issuesFound.title: newsletterForm email input missing aria-invalid on validation
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter email field uses inlineValidator.bind() which calls srUtils.markInvalid(). When validation fails, the field lacks aria-invalid="true", preventing screen readers from announcing the error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3128: Ensure srUtils.markInvalid() is updated per Issue 4, or add `setAttr(_emailEl, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` in the _onSubmit validation error handler before calling inlineValidator.validate().

issuesFound.title: contactForm full_name input missing aria-invalid on validation
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form full_name field uses inlineValidator.bind() with required and minLength rules. When validation fails, srUtils.markInvalid() does not set aria-invalid="true", leaving screen reader users unaware of the error.

issuesFound.title: contactForm email input missing aria-invalid on validation
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form email field uses inlineValidator validation. When invalid, the field does not receive aria-invalid="true" due to the missing logic in srUtils.markInvalid().

issuesFound.title: contactForm consent checkbox missing aria-invalid on validation
issuesFound.explanation: The consent checkbox validates via inlineValidator with the 'checked' rule but does not receive aria-invalid="true" when unchecked.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3220: Ensure srUtils.markInvalid() sets aria-invalid="true" as per Issue 4.

issuesFound.title: multiStepWizard wizard_name input missing aria-invalid on validation
issuesFound.explanation: The wizard name field in step 1 uses inlineValidator.bind() but does not get aria-invalid="true" set when validation fails.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3370: Ensure srUtils.markInvalid() sets aria-invalid="true" as per Issue 4.

issuesFound.title: multiStepWizard wizard_email input missing aria-invalid on validation
issuesFound.explanation: The wizard email field validates via inlineValidator but lacks aria-invalid="true" on error due to the utility function issue.

issuesFound.title: multiStepWizard wizard_plan input missing aria-invalid on validation

issuesFound.title: multiStepWizard wizard_card_name input missing aria-invalid on validation

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer not removed from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The close() function hides the mobile navigation drawer by removing CSS classes but does not set aria-hidden="true" on the drawer element. If the CSS uses off-screen positioning rather than display:none, the drawer remains accessible to screen readers when closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1695: Add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` in the close() function after `removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);` and add `_drawer.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');` in the open() function.

issuesFound.title: stickyHeader not removed from accessibility tree when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The stickyHeader.hide() function adds a CSS class to hide the header but does not set aria-hidden="true". If the CSS uses transform or opacity rather than display:none, the header remains in the accessibility tree when visually hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1848: Add `setAttr(_header, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` in the hide() function and `removeAttr(_header, 'aria-hidden');` in the show() function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The close() function accepts an announceToSR parameter but never calls liveRegion.announce() even when the parameter is true. Screen reader users are not notified when the navigation menu closes, particularly when it closes automatically on breakpoint change or outside click.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1695: Add inside the close() function: `if (announceToSR !== false) { liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed'); }`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.close() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The close() function collapses the FAQ answer panel but does not announce the state change via the live region, even when announceToSR is true. Users navigating with screen readers may not realize the panel has collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2700: Add inside the close() function before the emit call: `if (announceToSR !== false) { liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.faqClosed || '{q} — collapsed').replace('{q}', truncate(item.question, 60))); }`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() missing focus management
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, focus remains on the trigger button instead of moving to the drawer or its first focusable element. Keyboard users must manually navigate into the drawer, violating WCAG focus management requirements for modal dialogs.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1688: Add after opening the drawer: `const firstFocusable = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0]; if (firstFocusable) firstFocusable.focus({ preventScroll: true });`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() fails to set aria-expanded="true"

issuesFound.title: fileUploadField validation failure omits aria-invalid

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel.play() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Starting automatic slide rotation changes the carousel behavior and button state, but no announcement confirms the action to screen reader users. Users may be confused by the sudden auto-advancement.
issuesFound.fix: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.carouselPlaying || 'Automatic slide rotation started');` at the end of the play function.

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel.pause() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Pausing the carousel stops auto-advancement but does not inform screen reader users that rotation has been paused, leaving them uncertain about the current state.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() missing live region announcement

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Closing a dropdown menu collapses the panel and returns focus to the trigger, but the closed state is not announced via a live region, potentially leaving users unaware the menu has disappeared.
issuesFound.fix: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Dropdown closed');` before the `emit` call in the _closeInstance function.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch._close() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Dismissing the search suggestion panel (via Escape or selection) hides the listbox but does not announce the closure to screen reader users, who may expect the suggestions to still be available.
issuesFound.fix: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.searchClosed || 'Search suggestions closed');` at the end of the _close function.

issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is closed, it is hidden via CSS class removal but remains in the accessibility tree because aria-hidden="true" is never applied. Screen readers may still access the visually hidden content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1780: Add `_drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');` before `_unlockScroll();`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() lacks aria-hidden removal
issuesFound.explanation: When the drawer is opened, the previously set aria-hidden="true" is not removed, leaving the content inaccessible to screen readers despite being visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1766: Add `_drawer.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');` immediately after `_lockScroll();`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() lacks live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Opening a dropdown menu changes the visible state but provides no announcement to screen readers, leaving users unaware that content has appeared.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1849: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');` immediately after the emit() call

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance() lacks live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Closing a dropdown menu removes content from view without announcing the state change, causing confusion for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1863: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');` immediately after the emit() call

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() lacks focus management
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile menu opens, focus remains on the trigger button instead of moving to the drawer or the first focusable element within it, violating WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1768: Add `const firstFocusable = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0]; if (firstFocusable) firstFocusable.focus();` before the closing brace

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() lacks focus return
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile menu closes, focus is not returned to the trigger button that opened it, leaving keyboard users stranded at the bottom of the page or in an undefined location.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1782: Add `_trigger.focus();` immediately after `_unlockScroll();`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav lacks focus trap implementation
issuesFound.explanation: The module declares a _trap variable for focus trapping but never instantiates or activates it. Keyboard users can tab out of the open mobile menu and access background content, violating modal dialog behavior expectations.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1767: Add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger }); _trap.activate();` and Line 1781: Add `if (_trap) { _trap.deactivate(); _trap = null; }`

issuesFound.title: Contact first name input missing autocomplete
issuesFound.explanation: The input selector '#contact-first-name' identifies a field collecting personal data (first name) but the code does not ensure it has autocomplete="given-name", preventing browsers from assisting users with autofill.
issuesFound.fix: Line 411: In the HTML element selected by this selector, add attribute `autocomplete="given-name"` or set it via JS when binding the field: `field.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'given-name');`

issuesFound.title: Contact last name input missing autocomplete
issuesFound.explanation: The input selector '#contact-last-name' identifies a field collecting personal data (last name) but lacks autocomplete="family-name".
issuesFound.fix: Line 412: Add `autocomplete="family-name"` to the HTML element or set via JS.

issuesFound.title: Contact email input missing autocomplete
issuesFound.explanation: The input selector '#contact-email' identifies an email field but lacks autocomplete="email".
issuesFound.fix: Line 413: Add `autocomplete="email"` to the HTML element or set via JS.

issuesFound.title: Contact company input missing autocomplete
issuesFound.explanation: The input selector '#contact-company' identifies an organization field but lacks autocomplete="organization".
issuesFound.fix: Line 414: Add `autocomplete="organization"` to the HTML element or set via JS.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter email input missing autocomplete
issuesFound.explanation: The input selector '#newsletter-email' identifies an email field but lacks autocomplete="email".
issuesFound.fix: Line 404: Add `autocomplete="email"` to the HTML element or set via JS when initializing the form.

issuesFound.title: Wizard name input missing autocomplete
issuesFound.explanation: The input selector '[name="wizard_name"]' collects personal name data but lacks autocomplete="name".
issuesFound.fix: Line 3010: Add `autocomplete="name"` to the HTML element or set via JS in the field configuration.

issuesFound.title: Wizard email input missing autocomplete
issuesFound.explanation: The input selector '[name="wizard_email"]' collects email data but lacks autocomplete="email".
issuesFound.fix: Line 3011: Add `autocomplete="email"` to the HTML element or set via JS.

issuesFound.title: Wizard card name input missing autocomplete
issuesFound.explanation: The input selector '[name="wizard_card_name"]' collects credit card holder name but lacks autocomplete="cc-name".
issuesFound.fix: Line 3015: Add `autocomplete="cc-name"` to the HTML element or set via JS.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer missing aria-modal
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer behaves as a modal (blocks interaction with background content) but lacks aria-modal="true", causing screen readers to not treat it as a modal dialog.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1767: Add `_drawer.setAttribute('aria-modal', 'true');` when opening the drawer, and remove it in close().

issuesFound.title: discountCodeInput status not linked to input
issuesFound.explanation: The status element for discount code feedback is created but not associated with the input field via aria-describedby, so screen readers may not announce the error/success message when the input is focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2380: After creating _statusEl, add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-describedby', srUtils.ensureId(_statusEl));`
