issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu instance functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu open and close functions manage panel visibility and CSS classes, but fail to update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. This prevents assistive technologies from announcing the dropdown state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1250: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` at the end of _openInstance function.
  Line 1260: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` at the end of _closeInstance function.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When opening an FAQ item, the function animates the panel and updates classes but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. While the close function sets it to false, the open function must set it to true to maintain state consistency.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2100: Insert `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` inside the open function after `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab buttons missing aria-pressed updates
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons function as toggle buttons indicating the active filter state, but the code only toggles CSS classes without updating aria-pressed. Screen readers cannot identify which filter is currently active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1800: Inside the forEach loop, add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close missing live region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu opens or closes, no announcement is made to screen readers. Users navigating with assistive technology may not be aware that content has appeared or disappeared.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1250: Insert `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');` at the end of _openInstance.
  Line 1260: Insert `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');` at the end of _closeInstance.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open/close missing live region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ panel expand and collapse actions are not announced to screen readers. Users may not realize that answer content has been revealed or hidden.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2100: Insert `liveRegion.announce(item.question + ' expanded');` inside the open function.
  Line 2120: Insert `liveRegion.announce(item.question + ' collapsed');` inside the close function.

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: After applying filters, the product grid updates and result counts change, but no announcement is made to screen readers about the new results count or filter status.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1750: Insert `liveRegion.announce(`Showing ${totalMatches} products`);` at the end of applyFilters after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When switching billing periods (monthly/annual), prices update visually but the change is not announced to screen readers, leaving users unaware of the price change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1950: Insert `liveRegion.announce('Prices switched to ' + period + ' billing');` inside setPeriod after `_syncToggleState(period);`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The utility function marks a field as invalid by linking an error message via aria-describedby, but fails to set aria-invalid="true". Screen readers rely on this attribute to identify invalid fields.
issuesFound.fix: Line 800: Insert `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` inside markInvalid function.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid missing aria-invalid attribute reset
issuesFound.explanation: When clearing validation errors, the function removes the error description but does not set aria-invalid="false" or remove the attribute, leaving the field incorrectly marked as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 810: Insert `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` inside markValid function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button lacks an aria-controls attribute pointing to the drawer element. This prevents screen readers from identifying which element is controlled by the button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1200: Insert `if (_drawer) setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', _drawer.id || 'mobile-nav-drawer');` inside init function after verifying _drawer exists.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer missing aria-hidden when closed
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is closed, it remains in the accessibility tree because aria-hidden is not set to true. Screen readers can still access the hidden content.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1170: Insert `if (_drawer) setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` inside close function.
  Line 1150: Insert `if (_drawer) setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'false');` inside open function.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion non-native triggers missing keyboard activation
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ trigger elements that are not native buttons (e.g., divs with role="button") can be focused but cannot be activated with Enter or Space keys because the keydown handler only manages arrow navigation, not activation.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance and _closeInstance do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu controller initializes aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button during registration but never updates it when the dropdown opens or closes. The _openInstance and _closeInstance functions manipulate panel visibility without setting aria-expanded to reflect the current state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3685: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` in _openInstance after `inst.open = true;`
  Line 3695: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` in _closeInstance after `inst.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button lacks an aria-controls attribute pointing to the drawer element's id, failing to establish the required programmatic relationship between the trigger and the controlled region.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3579: After ensuring _drawer has an id (e.g., `srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav')`), add:
  `if (_drawer.id && !_trigger.getAttribute('aria-controls')) setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, _drawer.id);`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When form validation fails, the markInvalid function associates the error message with the field via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the input element. Screen readers cannot identify that the field contains an invalid value.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2012: After `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`, add:
  `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When a field transitions from invalid to valid, the markValid function removes the error message from aria-describedby but does not remove the aria-invalid attribute or set it to "false". Screen readers may continue to announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2029: After the aria-describedby cleanup block, add:
  `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` or `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);`

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu state changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown menus open or close, no announcement is made to screen reader users via the liveRegion announcer. Users are not informed when the dropdown panel appears or disappears.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3688: In _openInstance, after opening the panel, add:
  `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Dropdown menu opened');`
  Line 3698: In _closeInstance, after closing the panel, add:
  `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Dropdown menu closed');`

issuesFound.title: Active filter chips updates not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When filter chips are added to or removed from the active filters container in response to user actions, the change is not announced via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed of the current active filter state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 4215: At the end of the render() function (before the closing brace), add:
  `if (chips.length) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.filtersUpdated || `Active filters: ${chips.map(c => c.label).join(', ')}`);`

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav drawer not removed from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is closed, it is only hidden via CSS class removal but remains in the accessibility tree without aria-hidden="true". Screen readers may still navigate to the invisible drawer content.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3546: After `removeClass(document.body, CSS.navOpen);`, add:
  `_drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');`
  Line 3524: In open(), after adding classes, add:
  `_drawer.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');`

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav does not return focus to trigger on close
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation menu is closed (via Escape key, outside click, or toggle), focus is not programmatically returned to the trigger button. Keyboard users lose their place in the focus order.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3550: Before the emit call in close(), add:
  `if (document.activeElement && _drawer.contains(document.activeElement)) { _trigger.focus(); }`

issuesFound.title: File upload validation errors do not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When file validation fails (e.g., file too large or wrong type), the error is announced via liveRegion.announceAssertive but the file input element is not marked with aria-invalid="true", so the error state is not persistently exposed to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 4805: In the validation failure block (where `liveRegion.announceAssertive` is called), add:
  `setAttr(binding.inputEl, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');`
  Line 4810: When validation passes and file is added successfully, add:
  `setAttr(binding.inputEl, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Pricing comparison table toggle aria-expanded not initialized
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing module may contain a comparison table toggle button (data-expand-comparison) that is collapsed by default, but its aria-expanded attribute is not initialized to "false" on page load.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3915: In init(), after setting up the pricing toggle, add:
  `const compTrigger = qs('[data-expand-comparison]'); if (compTrigger && !compTrigger.getAttribute('aria-expanded')) setAttr(compTrigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

fpTitles: Mobile nav does not return focus to trigger on close

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `close` function removes the open CSS classes from the drawer but does not set `aria-expanded="false"` on the trigger button (`_trigger`), leaving screen readers unaware that the menu is collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2613: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after `removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init does not initialise aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `init` function selects the trigger button (`_trigger`) and drawer but does not set an initial `aria-expanded="false"` state on page load when the menu is closed, violating the requirement that ARIA states reflect the initial state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2672: Add `if (_trigger && !_drawer.classList.contains(CSS.open)) setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after `_initialised = true;`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer not removed from accessibility tree when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The `close` function hides the drawer (`_drawer`) by removing a CSS class but does not set `aria-hidden="true"` or re-apply the `hidden` attribute. The drawer remains in the accessibility tree with visible content even when visually hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2613: Add `_drawer.setAttribute('hidden', '');` and `_drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');` when closing. Line 2589: Add `_drawer.removeAttribute('hidden');` and `_drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'false');` when opening.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance` function reveals the dropdown panel (`inst.panel`) but does not set `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button (`inst.trigger`), leaving screen readers unaware the dropdown is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2835: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` after `inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _closeInstance does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `_closeInstance` function hides the dropdown panel but does not set `aria-expanded="false"` on the trigger button, so screen readers continue to announce the dropdown as expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2855: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` when closing the panel.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not set aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` function expands the FAQ panel (`item.panel`) and removes the `hidden` attribute, but fails to set `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button (`item.trigger`). Only the `close` function sets it to false.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5360: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` after `item.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not set aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` function marks fields as invalid by adding CSS classes and updating `aria-describedby`, but never sets `aria-invalid="true"` on invalid fields. Screen readers cannot convey the invalid state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 6460: Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` in the else branch (invalid case). Line 6452: Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` in the valid case.

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod does not announce billing period change
issuesFound.explanation: The `setPeriod` function updates prices and UI state when switching between monthly/annual billing, but does not announce the change via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed that prices have updated or which billing period is now active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4772: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingPeriodChanged || 'Switched to ' + period + ' billing');` after updating the UI.

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters does not announce results
issuesFound.explanation: The `applyFilters` function updates the product grid and count badge when filtering, but does not announce the number of results or that filtering has completed. Screen reader users receive no feedback that the product list has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4075: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.filterApplied || 'Showing ' + totalMatches + ' products');` inside the filterAnimation callback.

fpTitles: faqAccordion open function does not set aria-expanded

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger elements
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu controller opens and closes panels via _openInstance and _closeInstance, but fails to synchronize the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. This prevents assistive technologies from announcing the expanded/collapsed state of the dropdown.
issuesFound.fix: Line _openInstance function: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` immediately after setting `inst.open = true;`. Line _closeInstance function: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` immediately after setting `inst.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on combobox input
issuesFound.explanation: The search suggestion combobox is expanded and collapsed via _open and _close functions, but the aria-expanded attribute on the search input is never updated. Screen readers cannot inform users when the suggestion list is visible or hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line _open function: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` immediately after setting `_searchState.open = true;`. Line _close function: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` immediately after setting `_searchState.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab buttons do not update aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The category filter tab buttons toggle active state via CSS classes but fail to update aria-pressed. Without this attribute, screen readers cannot convey which filter category is currently active.
issuesFound.fix: Line _syncTabAriaStates function: Inside the forEach loop after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`, add `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));`.

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The validation utility marks fields as invalid by linking error messages via aria-describedby, but never sets aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields or aria-invalid="false" on valid fields. Assistive technologies cannot identify which fields have failed validation.
issuesFound.fix: Line markInvalid function: After `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`, add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');`. Line markValid function: After checking if field exists, add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` (or `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);`).

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing initial aria-expanded at page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button is created in the DOM without an initial aria-expanded attribute. Since the navigation is closed by default on page load, the button must have aria-expanded="false" set during initialization so screen readers announce the correct initial state.
issuesFound.fix: Line init function: After setting `_initialised = true;` and before attaching event listeners, add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu triggers missing initial aria-expanded at page load
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown triggers are registered, they are given aria-haspopup and aria-controls attributes, but the initial aria-expanded="false" is not set. Screen readers will not announce the collapsed state of the menu until the first interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line register function: After `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, panelId);` and before `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`, add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing initial aria-expanded at page load
issuesFound.explanation: The search combobox input is configured with role, aria-haspopup, and aria-controls, but lacks the initial aria-expanded="false" attribute required to indicate that the suggestion list is initially collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line init function: After `const listbox = suggestionListRenderer.mount(_inputEl);`, add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button controls the navigation drawer element but does not have an aria-controls attribute referencing the drawer's ID. Screen readers cannot establish the programmatic relationship between the button and the controlled content.
issuesFound.fix: Line init function: After `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` (or in the same location), add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, _drawer.id || srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'nav-drawer'));`.

issuesFound.title: pricing period changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Switching between monthly and annual billing periods updates visible prices but does not announce the change to screen readers. Users may not realize the pricing display has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line setPeriod function: After `_syncToggleState(period);` and before `emit(document, 'pricing:periodchange', ...);`, add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'month' ? STRINGS.pricingMonthly : STRINGS.pricingAnnual);`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav state changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The open and close functions accept an announceToSR parameter but never use it to trigger live region announcements. When the navigation opens or closes, screen reader users receive no confirmation of the state change.
issuesFound.fix: Line open function: Before `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:open', ...);`, add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');`. Line close function: Before `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:close', ...);`, add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing dropdown menus changes the visible content but provides no aria-live announcement. Screen reader users are not informed when a submenu has expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line _openInstance function: Before `emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:open', ...);`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.menuOpened || 'Dropdown menu opened');`. Line _closeInstance function: Before `emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:close', ...);`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.menuClosed || 'Dropdown menu closed');`.

issuesFound.title: activeFilterChips updates not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When filter chips are added or removed, the render function updates the DOM but does not announce the change. Screen reader users are not notified that the active filter set has been modified.
issuesFound.fix: Line render function: At the end of the function after appending chips to the container, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.filtersUpdated || 'Active filters updated');`.

fpTitles: activeFilterChips updates not announced via live region

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown menu instances are opened or closed, the code updates the panel's hidden attribute and CSS classes but fails to update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. This breaks the ARIA disclosure pattern and prevents screen readers from announcing the state change.
issuesFound.fix:
Function dropdownMenu._openInstance() (after `inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`): `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`
  Function dropdownMenu._closeInstance() (before `emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:close', ...)`): `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`
  Function dropdownMenu.register() (after `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`): `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function missing aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: While the close() function correctly sets aria-expanded="false" on the FAQ trigger, the open() function fails to set aria-expanded="true" when expanding the panel. This leaves assistive technologies unaware that the accordion panel is now expanded and content is visible.
issuesFound.fix: Function faqAccordion.open() (after `item.open = true;`): `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The search input acts as an ARIA combobox controlling a suggestion listbox. When the suggestion panel opens or closes, the code fails to update aria-expanded on the input element to communicate the popup state, violating the combobox pattern requirements.
issuesFound.fix:
Function siteSearch._open() (after `_searchState.open = true;`): `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`
  Function siteSearch._close() (after `_searchState.open = false;`): `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`
  Function siteSearch.init() (after `suggestionListRenderer.mount(_inputEl);`): `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: pricing billing toggle buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The monthly/annual billing period buttons constitute a two-state toggle pattern where one is always pressed/active. The code toggles CSS classes but does not set aria-pressed on the buttons, preventing screen readers from conveying which billing period is currently selected.
issuesFound.fix:
Function pricing._syncToggleState() (inside forEach, after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`): `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));`
  Function pricing.init() (after `_periodBtns.forEach(...)` binding): Add initial `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', 'false');` for inactive buttons and `'true'` for the active one.

issuesFound.title: productFilter filter tabs missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The category filter tab buttons toggle between active and inactive states but lack aria-pressed attributes. As these operate as toggle buttons (not a tablist with aria-selected), they require aria-pressed to communicate their active/inactive state to assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: Validation functions not setting aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When form fields are marked as invalid or valid, these functions update visual styling and aria-describedby associations but fail to set aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields or remove/set aria-invalid="false" on valid fields. Screen readers cannot announce the validation state to users.
issuesFound.fix:
Function srUtils.markInvalid() (after `addDescribedBy(control, errorEl);`): `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`
  Function srUtils.markValid() (after removing describedby): `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav initial aria-expanded not set
issuesFound.explanation: On initial page load, the mobile navigation trigger button lacks aria-expanded="false" even though the menu is initially closed. ARIA states must reflect the current state immediately when the page loads, not only after the first interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Function mobileNav.init() (after `_trigger.addEventListener('click', _onTriggerClick);`): `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu initial aria-expanded not set
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown triggers are registered, they receive aria-haspopup and aria-controls but lack the initial aria-expanded="false" attribute required to indicate that the controlled panel is initially collapsed on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Function dropdownMenu.register() (after `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`): `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch initial aria-expanded not set
issuesFound.explanation: The search combobox input lacks the initial aria-expanded="false" attribute required to indicate the initially collapsed state of the suggestion panel when the page loads.
issuesFound.fix: Function siteSearch.init() (after `setAttr(input, 'role', 'combobox');` in mount or in init itself): `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: productFilter dynamic updates not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When users apply filters, the product grid updates dynamically showing or hiding cards, but no aria-live announcement informs screen reader users of the result count or that filtering has completed. Users are left unaware that the content has changed.
issuesFound.fix:
Function productFilter.applyFilters() (inside filterAnimation.run callback, after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`):
  ```
  liveRegion.announce(
    (STRINGS.filterApplied || 'Showing {n} products')
      .replace('{n}', String(totalMatches))
      .replace('{cat}', _productFilterState.activeCategory)
  );
  ```

issuesFound.title: pricing period changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch between monthly and annual billing, the price values update dynamically but screen reader users receive no confirmation of the billing period change or the new prices. This dynamic content change should be announced via a live region.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer not hidden from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is closed, it remains in the DOM with off-screen CSS positioning but lacks aria-hidden="true", meaning screen reader users can still navigate into the visually hidden off-canvas menu.
issuesFound.fix:
Function mobileNav.close() (after `removeClass(_drawer, CSS.open);`): `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');`
  Function mobileNav.open() (after `addClass(_drawer, ...)`): `_drawer.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu focus not returned to trigger on close
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu is closed via Escape key or outside click, keyboard focus is not returned to the trigger button that opened it. This leaves focus stranded or lost in the page, violating focus management requirements for disclosure widgets.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus not returned to trigger on close
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is closed, focus is not programmatically returned to the hamburger trigger button. Keyboard users are left without focus context, requiring them to navigate back to the trigger manually.
issuesFound.fix: Function mobileNav.close() (at end of function, after `_unlockScroll();`): `if (_trigger) _trigger.focus();`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus not moved to drawer on open
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation opens, focus remains on the trigger button instead of moving to the navigation drawer or the first focusable element within it. This prevents keyboard users from immediately accessing the newly revealed navigation content.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer is controlled by open(), close(), and toggle() functions, but none of these update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to reflect the current visibility state. Screen readers cannot announce whether the navigation menu is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line open function: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `addClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`
  Line close function: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after `removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`
  Line init function: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after the null check for _trigger and _drawer.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The search input functions as a combobox that displays a suggestion list, but the _open and _close functions do not toggle aria-expanded on the input element. Screen reader users cannot perceive when the suggestion list is visible or hidden.
issuesFound.fix:
Line _open: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `_searchState.open = true;`
  Line _close: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `_searchState.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: pricing switch missing aria-checked updates
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle element is assigned role="switch" during initialisation, but the setPeriod function never updates aria-checked to reflect the current state (e.g., true for annual, false for monthly). Screen readers cannot announce the switch state.
issuesFound.fix: Line setPeriod: Add `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', String(period === 'year'));` after `_syncToggleState(period);` (assuming 'year' represents the checked state).

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: When marking a form field as invalid, the function updates aria-describedby to link the error message but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen readers cannot identify that the field contains invalid input.
issuesFound.fix: Line markInvalid: Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid missing aria-invalid clearance
issuesFound.explanation: When clearing the invalid state from a field, the function removes the error reference from aria-describedby but does not remove the aria-invalid attribute or set it to "false". The field remains marked as invalid to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line markValid: Add `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');` or `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` after the aria-describedby cleanup logic.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search input is configured as a combobox with aria-controls and aria-haspopup, but aria-expanded is not initialised to "false" when the suggestion list is closed on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line mount: Add `setAttr(input, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `setAttr(input, 'aria-haspopup', 'listbox');`

issuesFound.title: productFilter missing live region announcement for results
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the product grid updates and the count badge changes visually, but there is no announcement via the live region system to inform screen reader users how many products are now displayed or if the filter returned no results.
issuesFound.fix: Line applyFilters: After `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`, add `liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);`

issuesFound.title: pricing module missing live region announcement for billing period change
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch between monthly and annual billing, the prices update visually with animation, but the change in billing period and the new prices are not announced to screen readers via the live region system.
issuesFound.fix: Line setPeriod: After `_updatePlanCard(plan, period);` (inside the forEach), or after the loop, add `liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label} billing`);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing live region announcement for open/close state
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer opens and closes without announcing the state change to screen readers, despite the functions accepting an announceToSR parameter that is never used to trigger a live region announcement.
issuesFound.fix:
Line open: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');` after `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:open', { drawer: _drawer });`
  Line close: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');` after `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:close', { drawer: _drawer });`

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop click handler missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When the scroll to top button is activated via click, the page scrolls to the top but the action is not announced to screen readers, unlike the keyboard shortcut (Alt+T) which does announce the action.
issuesFound.fix: Line _onClick: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.scrolledToTop || 'Scrolled to top of page');` before or after the scroll operation.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open function missing focus management
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, focus remains on the trigger button instead of moving to the drawer content or the first focusable element within it. Keyboard users cannot immediately navigate the newly opened menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line open: After `_lockScroll();`, add `const firstFocusable = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0] || _drawer; if (firstFocusable) firstFocusable.focus();`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close function missing focus return
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer closes, focus is not returned to the trigger button that opened it. Keyboard users lose their place in the tab order.
issuesFound.fix: Line close: Before or after `_unlockScroll();`, add `if (_trigger) _trigger.focus();` (ensure this happens before the drawer is hidden if possible, or after).

issuesFound.title: Newsletter email field error message not rendered and aria-describedby broken
issuesFound.explanation: The FormGroup component is called with the prop `error` instead of `errorMessage`, and the `errorId` prop is missing. FormGroup expects `errorMessage` to display text and `errorId` to render the ErrorMessage element with that ID. Consequently, the TextInput's aria-describedby references 'newsletter-email-error', but no element exists with that ID because ErrorMessage was never rendered. Screen readers will not announce the validation error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1420: <FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="newsletter-email" errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined} errorId="newsletter-email-error">

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav current page link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The active navigation link (with `item.current` true) receives a visual modifier class but no `aria-current="page"`. Screen reader users cannot identify which page is current within the navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1071: Add `aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined}` to the `<a>` element.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav panel navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The `<nav className="mobile-nav__body">` inside the mobile panel lacks an aria-label, making it indistinguishable from other navigation regions when using screen reader landmark navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1194: `<nav aria-label="Mobile" className="mobile-nav__body">`

issuesFound.title: MobileNav close button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton used to close the mobile navigation panel has no `aria-label` prop, violating the component's own TypeScript interface and leaving the button without an accessible name for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1202: `<IconButton aria-label="Close navigation" onClick={onClose} className="mobile-nav__close">`

issuesFound.title: MobileNav section toggle button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The button controlling the expandable section in mobile navigation uses `isExpanded` state and `aria-controls` but omits `aria-expanded`, preventing screen reader users from knowing the subsection's visibility state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1214: Add `aria-expanded={isExpanded}` to the button element.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav current page link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The active link in mobile navigation (with `item.current` true) lacks `aria-current="page"`, depriving screen reader users of orientation about their current location.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1232: Add `aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined}` to the `<a>` element.

issuesFound.title: HeroSection landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The `<section className="hero-section">` contains a heading with `id="hero-heading"` but the section lacks `aria-labelledby`, preventing it from being identified as a labelled landmark region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1244: `<section aria-labelledby="hero-heading" className="hero-section">`

issuesFound.title: StatsBar landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The `<section className="stats-bar">` contains a heading with `id="stats-heading"` but the section itself lacks `aria-labelledby`, failing to expose the region's purpose to screen reader landmark navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1316: `<section aria-labelledby="stats-heading" className="stats-bar">`

issuesFound.title: IntegrationsBar decorative clone list not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The duplicated `<ul>` list used for the seamless visual marquee loop lacks `aria-hidden="true"`. Screen readers encounter duplicate content, announcing the integration partners twice.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1362: `<ul role="list" className="integrations-bar__list" aria-hidden="true">`

issuesFound.title: StarRating decorative star characters not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The individual star characters (`★` and `☆`) are rendered as text content inside spans without `aria-hidden`. The parent container provides an aria-label, but the star characters are still read as "black star" or "white star" by some screen readers, creating redundant chatter.
issuesFound.fix: Line 854: `<span key={i} aria-hidden="true" className={...}>`

issuesFound.title: PlanCard article missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The `<article>` element in PlanCard contains a heading with a generated ID (`headingId`) but lacks `aria-labelledby`, preventing screen reader users from identifying the card's purpose when navigating by region or article.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1725: `<article aria-labelledby={headingId} className="pricing-card__article">`

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle monthly button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The monthly billing toggle button tracks selection state (`value === 'monthly'`) but lacks `aria-pressed`, leaving screen reader users unaware of which billing period is active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1693: Add `aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'}` to the button element.

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle annual button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The annual billing toggle button tracks selection state (`value === 'annual'`) but lacks `aria-pressed`, preventing assistive technology from conveying the active selection to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1700: Add `aria-pressed={value === 'annual'}` to the button element.

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel non-visible slides missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: Testimonial slides that are not currently visible (`isVisible === false`) remain in the accessibility tree. Screen reader users navigating by headings or regions encounter content from hidden slides, causing confusion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1600: Add `aria-hidden={!isVisible}` to the slide div element.

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel previous button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton for navigating to the previous slide contains only the character `‹` and lacks an `aria-label` prop, leaving the button without an accessible name for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1612: `<IconButton aria-label="Previous testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handlePrev} className="testimonials-track__prev">`

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel next button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton for navigating to the next slide contains only the character `›` and lacks an `aria-label` prop, making the button's purpose unidentifiable to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1617: `<IconButton aria-label="Next testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handleNext} className="testimonials-track__next">`

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy during loading state
issuesFound.explanation: When `isLoading` is true, the button displays a spinner but does not set `aria-busy="true"`. Screen reader users are not informed that the button is processing and unavailable for interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 880: Add `aria-busy={isLoading || undefined}` to the button element.

issuesFound.title: Button spinner decorative element not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The loading spinner span (`<span className="btn-spinner" />`) is decorative but lacks `aria-hidden`, causing screen readers to potentially announce it as an empty group or generic element.
issuesFound.fix: Line 892: `<span aria-hidden="true" className="btn-spinner" />`

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm submit button missing aria-busy
issuesFound.explanation: The submit button uses `loading={isSubmitting}` to show a spinner but does not receive `aria-busy`, failing to communicate the submitting state to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1864: Add `aria-busy={isSubmitting}` to the Button element.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm submit button missing aria-busy
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form submit button displays a loading state via `loading={isSubmitting}` but lacks `aria-busy`, leaving screen reader users unaware that the form is submitting.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1986: Add `aria-busy={isSubmitting}` to the Button element.

issuesFound.title: FormGroup required field marker announces bare asterisk
issuesFound.explanation: The required field indicator (` *`) lacks `aria-hidden`, causing screen readers to announce "star" or "asterisk" separately from the visually hidden "(required)" text that follows.
issuesFound.fix: Line 952: ` *`
